title: Include branch name in reset prompt
description: The reset prompts (soft, hard, mixed) should include the current branch name, e.g. 'Hard reset main to' instead of just 'Hard reset to'. This gives the user clearer context about what they're resetting.
close_reason: Reset prompts now include the current branch name, e.g. 'Hard reset main to' instead of 'Hard reset to'

title: C-c C-n in log buffer should go to parent of current commit
description: When point is on a commit in the log buffer, pressing C-c C-n should navigate to the parent commit (if it has one). This enables easy traversal of commit history.
close_reason: Added madolt-log-goto-parent command bound to C-c C-n in madolt-log-mode-map. Navigates to the parent commit section using madolt-log--parent-hash. Errors on initial commits (no parent) or when parent is not visible.

title: hard reset in madolt isn't really hard - it leaves diffs
description:
The SQL translation for `reset` (registered as `reset-tables`) matched ALL reset commands including `reset --hard` and `reset --soft`. The generator stripped all flags starting with `-`, so `--hard`/`--soft` were silently discarded. This meant `dolt reset --hard HEAD` was SQL-translated to `CALL DOLT_RESET('HEAD')` (treating HEAD as a table name) instead of `CALL DOLT_RESET('--hard', 'HEAD')`. When `madolt-use-sql-server` was enabled, hard reset silently degraded to a mixed reset, leaving working set diffs intact.

Fix: Added dedicated `reset-hard` and `reset-soft` SQL translations that correctly pass the flags through to DOLT_RESET(). The `reset-tables` pattern now explicitly rejects `--hard` and `--soft` args.
close_reason: Fixed: added dedicated reset-hard and reset-soft SQL translations; reset-tables pattern now excludes --hard/--soft

title: Implement b x for branch reset like magit
description: Add branch reset command bound to b x in the branch transient. In magit, b x resets the current branch to a specified commit, like git reset --hard. The dolt equivalent is dolt reset --hard <commit>. Should prompt for a commit-ish, defaulting to the commit at point if available.
close_reason: Implemented b x branch reset command: resets current branch via dolt reset --hard, non-current branches via dolt branch -f. Added madolt-anything-modified-p helper. 6 new tests.

title: Commit-ish at point should be default for interactive prompts
description: When positioned over a commit line (e.g. in a log buffer), that commit should become the default for any interactive prompt requiring a commit-ish, such as initiating a merge, doing a branch reset, creating a new branch, etc. This matches magit's behavior of using the thing-at-point as the default.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-commit-at-point, madolt-branch-at-point, madolt-tag-at-point, madolt-branch-or-commit-at-point functions and updated all 15 interactive prompt call sites to use section-at-point as the default

title: SQL connection returns stale data after DOLT_MERGE conflict
description: After DOLT_MERGE fails due to autocommit conflict, the SQL connection session becomes corrupted. Every subsequent query returns stale data (e.g. (('1')) for any SELECT). madolt-connection-disconnect/reconnect does not fix it. The mysql process filter may have stale data that gets returned for new queries. Needs investigation of madolt-connection-query's output handling.
close_reason: Fixed two causes: (1) parse-batch-output used string-trim which stripped leading tabs, losing empty column values. Changed to string-trim-right. (2) Added output drain (accept-process-output + clear) before sending new queries to flush late-arriving data from previous queries.

title: Merge pretends to succeed and reports wrong branch name: 'Merged railway/main into 1'
description:
After merging a remote tracking branch, the success message shows 'Merged railway/main into 1' instead of the actual branch name (e.g. 'Merged railway/main into main'). The '1' likely comes from the exit code or a misparse of the merge result.

More importantly, the merge didn't actually do anything AFAICS.
close_reason: Merge conflict detection and reporting now works: detects conflicts from DOLT_MERGE result rows, disconnects to rollback, pops process buffer with clear message. Verified interactively on d4d. Conflict resolution flow tracked separately in madolt-fk4j.

title: madolt-process buffer should indent all output like magit-process does
description: The process buffer currently shows command output without indentation. magit-process indents all output lines under the command heading, making it easier to visually distinguish commands from their output. madolt-process should match this convention.
close_reason: Process buffer output now indented with 2 spaces like magit-process. Verified interactively in d4d.

title: fetch/push/pull don't offer existing remotes as an option
description: Two bugs: (1) fetch/push/pull p suffix silently used default remote without prompting when multiple exist - fixed by calling madolt-remote--read-remote. (2) SQL translation for remote-list used CONCAT(name, '\t', url) which produced literal backslash-t instead of tab - fixed by using SELECT name, url (separate columns joined by madolt--run-sql with real tabs).
close_reason: Fixed two bugs: (1) SQL translation for remote-list used literal backslash-t in CONCAT instead of separate columns, so madolt-remotes returned nil when SQL server was active. (2) e (elsewhere) suffix now always prompts via force-prompt flag, even with a single remote. Verified interactively in d4d context.

title: Show 'Refreshing madolt...' message during status refresh, then 'done'
description: When refreshing the status buffer (g), display 'Refreshing madolt...' in the minibuffer at the start, then append 'done' when the refresh completes. Matches magit's 'Refreshing magit...done' behavior.
close_reason: Added Refreshing madolt.../done messages

title: Refs buffer should default to visibility level 2
description: The refs buffer (y) should default to section visibility level 2 so that branch/tag sections are expanded by default, matching magit's behavior.
close_reason: Fixed in refs buffer commit: visibility level 2, ahead/behind layout, text properties, header space

title: Make SQL connection per-database instead of global
description: The SQL connection state (process, port, ready, db-dir, server-process) is stored in global defvars, but each dolt database needs its own sql-server on its own port. This causes cross-database connection misuse when working with multiple dolt repos. Refactor to use a hash table keyed by database directory or buffer-local state.
close_reason: Refactored: connection state now stored in a cl-defstruct per database directory, keyed by file-truename in a hash table

title: refreshing madolt-status buffer yields different results every time
description:
Root cause: madolt--run-cli and madolt--prefetch merged stderr with stdout via call-process and make-process. When a stale sql-server.info exists in a parent directory, dolt CLI prints connection error warnings to stderr with variable timing, contaminating the output. Additionally, when parallel prefetch processes fail due to dolt lock contention (6 processes compete for the same server lock), the failed results were cached, causing downstream parsers to get empty/error output.

Fix: (1) madolt--run-cli now redirects stderr to a temp file, only including it in output on non-zero exit. (2) madolt--prefetch no longer caches failed (non-zero exit) results, allowing madolt--run to retry synchronously. (3) madolt--prefetch uses round-robin accept-process-output on specific processes. (4) Added madolt-check-stale-sql-server to warn about dead server info files. (5) Fixed maphash non-determinism in madolt-refs--insert-remote-branches.
close_reason: Fixed: stderr separation in CLI runner and prefetch, failed prefetch results no longer cached (retry falls through to synchronous), stale sql-server.info detection with user warning, maphash nondeterminism in refs replaced with sorted dolist.

title: refs: preserve section visibility cache across refreshes
description: Magit adds magit-preserve-section-visibility-cache to kill-buffer-hook in refs buffers, ensuring expand/collapse state is preserved across buffer recreations. Madolt doesn't do this.
close_reason: Implemented visibility cache preservation: madolt-refs--preserve-visibility-cache saves to global hash on kill-buffer-hook, madolt-refs--restore-visibility-cache restores on buffer creation

title: refs: add margin support for date/author display
description: Magit has full margin support via magit-refs-margin customization -- can show committer/author date in the right margin with configurable style (age, date format). Also optionally shows author name. Tags have separate margin control. Madolt has no margin support in the refs buffer.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-refs-margin defcustom with full margin infrastructure: madolt-refs--margin-config, madolt-refs--setup-margin, margin overlays for branches/tags with age/date/author display. Mirrors magit-refs-margin.

title: refs: handle detached HEAD state
description: Magit shows a (commit HEAD) section with '(detached)' propertized with magit-branch-warning face when HEAD is detached. Madolt has no handling for this case.
close_reason: Dolt does not support detached HEAD state (dolt checkout <hash> fails with: 'dolt does not support a detached head state'). This issue is not applicable.

title: refs: use @ marker for HEAD comparison, not *
description: Magit uses @ when the focus ref is HEAD, * for named refs. Madolt always uses *. The @ indicator is part of the focus column concept. Should match magit's convention.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-refs--format-focus-column: uses @ for current branch when comparing against HEAD, * for the branch matching a named comparison target. Mirrors magit convention.

title: $ should switch to process buffer with point on last command
description: Currently `$` opens the process buffer but doesn't switch to it or position the point at the last command. It should both switch to the buffer and place point on the most recent command output.
close_reason: $ now uses madolt-display-buffer (selects window) and calls madolt--process-goto-last to position point on the last process section heading.

title: Fix infinite loop in madolt-diff--compute-value-widths when fields exceed window width
description: When a row has many columns whose key names alone exceed the available window width, the iterative shrinking loop in madolt-diff--compute-value-widths enters an infinite loop. The loop tries to reduce all value widths below 1 but (max 1 target) prevents it, causing a hang. Triggered by the organisations table (9 fields, 119 chars of key names vs 115 chars available). Fix: add (> (madolt-diff--vec-max widths) 1) guard to the while condition.
close_reason: Fixed by adding (> (madolt-diff--vec-max widths) 1) guard to the while loop condition in madolt-diff--compute-value-widths

title: Add show-more to log buffer (enhance existing limit)
description:
The log buffer already has a limit (madolt-log--limit defaults to 25, user can override via -n transient argument), but it does NOT show a "Type + to show more" button when truncated.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-log-refresh-buffer (madolt-log.el:190) calls madolt-log-entries with madolt-log--limit
2. **CLI command**: dolt log -n <N> [extra-args] [rev] — built at madolt-dolt.el:273
3. **Rendering**: madolt-log--insert-commit-section (madolt-log.el:207) per entry
4. **Limit**: madolt-log--limit defaults to 25 (line 94). User can override via -n transient argument (line 106-108). Extracted by madolt-log--extract-limit (line 164).

## What is missing

- No "Type + to show more" button when the log returns exactly N entries (indicating there are more)
- No + keybinding to double the limit
- No auto-more hook when cursor navigates to the end

## Implementation plan

After rendering all log entries, check if the count equals madolt-log--limit. If so, insert a show-more button section (using the shared infrastructure). Bind + in madolt-log-mode-map to double madolt-log--limit and refresh.

## Also applies to commit diff expansion

madolt-log--insert-commit-diff (madolt-log.el:316) calls madolt-diff-json with parent..hash to show the full diff when TAB is pressed on a log entry. This diff has NO row limit. It should be handled by the structured diff row limit bead.

## Also applies to revision buffer

madolt-revision-refresh-buffer (madolt-log.el:372) calls madolt-diff-json at line 418-420. Same unbounded diff. Also handled by the structured diff bead.

## Files affected

- madolt-log.el (show-more button after entries, + keybinding in log mode map)
close_reason: Implemented show-more button in log buffer. When entries equal the limit, a button appears. madolt-log-double-limit doubles the limit and refreshes. 3 new tests, all 35 log tests pass.

title: Add row limit to inline diffs in status buffer
description:
Inline diffs in the status buffer (expanded via TAB on a table entry) currently render ALL rows, which hangs Emacs on large tables.

## Current code path

1. **Entry point**: madolt--wash-table-diff (madolt-status.el:353) — used as a lazy washer for staged/unstaged/untracked table sections
2. **Called from**: madolt--insert-table-change-section (madolt-status.el:229) inserts via (magit-insert-section-body (madolt--wash-table-diff)) at line 249
3. **Delegates to**: madolt-diff-insert-table (madolt-diff.el:639) via (madolt-diff-insert-table table staged) at line 365
4. **CLI command**: dolt diff -r json [--staged] <table>
5. **Rendering**: Same madolt-diff--insert-row-diff loop at madolt-diff.el:671: (dolist (row-change data-diff) ...)
6. **No limit**: All rows from the single-table diff are rendered inline

## Implementation plan

The inline diff case needs a LOWER default limit than the full diff buffer (e.g. 20 vs 100) since it is embedded in the status buffer where performance matters most.

If the structured diff bead (madolt-diff--insert-table-diff truncation) is implemented first, this may be automatically handled by passing a smaller limit parameter. But the status buffer may need its own defcustom (madolt-diff-section-max-rows) to control the inline limit separately.

The show-more button should work within the inline section — pressing + should expand just that table's inline diff, not the whole status buffer.

## Files affected

- madolt-status.el (madolt--wash-table-diff, limit parameter passing)
- madolt-diff.el (madolt-diff-insert-table needs to accept/use a limit)
close_reason: Already implemented as part of madolt-7y7. madolt-diff-insert-table (called from madolt-status.el:365) uses madolt-diff-section-max-rows (20) to limit inline diffs, with show-more button when truncated.

title: Add row limit to structured diff (madolt-diff.el)
description:
Structured diff (JSON-based) currently fetches and renders ALL rows with no limit, which hangs Emacs on large tables.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-diff--refresh-structured (madolt-diff.el:247) calls madolt-diff-json (madolt-dolt.el:247-250)
2. **CLI command**: dolt diff -r json [args...]
3. **Rendering**: madolt-diff--insert-table-diff (madolt-diff.el:258) iterates data_diff array, calling madolt-diff--insert-row-diff (madolt-diff.el:490) per row
4. **The unbounded loop**: (dolist (row-change data-diff) ...) at line 282-283

## How dolt diff works

dolt diff supports --where= (already in the transient at line 149) but has NO --limit flag. The JSON diff cannot be limited at the CLI level. Truncation must happen post-fetch.

## Implementation plan

The best place to truncate is in madolt-diff--insert-table-diff where the (dolist (row-change data-diff) ...) loop runs. Replace with a counted loop:

1. Add a defcustom madolt-diff-max-rows (default 100 for diff buffer, 20 for inline)
2. In madolt-diff--insert-table-diff, use cl-loop with a counter:
   - Insert up to madolt-diff-max-rows rows
   - If data_diff has more rows than the limit, insert a show-more button section
   - Track the total count so the button knows how many are hidden
3. When + is pressed, double the limit and re-render

This single change protects ALL five callers of madolt-diff--insert-table-diff:
- madolt-diff--refresh-structured (diff buffer)
- madolt-diff-insert-table (status inline diff)
- madolt-log--insert-commit-diff (log TAB expansion)
- madolt-revision-refresh-buffer (revision buffer)
- Any future callers

## Also applies to raw diff mode

madolt-diff--refresh-raw (madolt-diff.el:558) calls madolt-diff-raw (madolt-dolt.el:257-260), then madolt-diff--insert-raw-table-section (line 594) inserts all lines from (split-string body "\n") at line 603. This needs similar post-fetch line counting and truncation.

## Files affected

- madolt-diff.el (madolt-diff--insert-table-diff, madolt-diff--insert-raw-table-section)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustom for limit)
close_reason: Implemented row limits for structured diff (100 rows), inline status diffs (20 rows), and raw diffs (200 lines). Show-more buttons with + keybinding. 8 new tests, all 51 diff tests pass.

title: Row-limit infrastructure: show-more section, defcustoms, + keybinding
description:
Create the shared infrastructure for row-limiting across all madolt operations, following the magit "Type + to show more" pattern.

## Magit pattern (from magit-log.el)

Magit uses this approach in magit-log-wash-log (magit-log.el ~line 1383):

1. A counter magit-log-count starts at 0
2. Each commit washed by magit-log-wash-rev increments it
3. After washing all commits, compare magit-log-count to magit-log-get-commit-limit
4. If they are equal (meaning git returned exactly as many as the limit, so there are probably more), insert a "show more" button
5. The button is a magit-insert-section with type `longer` containing an insert-text-button
6. This only happens in magit-log-mode and magit-reflog-mode (full log buffers), not in status buffer log sections

The button text is generated dynamically using substitute-command-keys with \<magit-log-mode-map>\[magit-log-double-commit-limit] — this resolves to the actual key bound to the function ("+"). So if the keybinding changes, the button text updates automatically.

When user presses +, magit-log-double-commit-limit calls magit-log-set-commit-limit with '* (multiply), which finds the current -n<N> in magit-buffer-log-args, doubles it, replaces the arg, and calls magit-refresh.

magit also has magit-log-maybe-show-more-commits on magit-section-movement-hook — when magit-log-auto-more is non-nil and cursor lands on the longer section via section movement, it auto-triggers doubling.

## Implementation plan for madolt

1. **Defcustoms** (in madolt-dolt.el or a new madolt-limits.el):
   - madolt-diff-section-max-rows (default 20) — for inline diffs in status buffer
   - madolt-diff-max-rows (default 100) — for diff buffer
   - madolt-log-max-entries (already exists as madolt-log--limit = 25)
   - madolt-reflog-max-entries (default 100)
   - madolt-blame-max-rows (default 100)
   - madolt-sql-max-rows (default 100)

2. **Show-more section helper** (in madolt-mode.el):
   A reusable function that inserts a magit-insert-section of type `longer` with an insert-text-button, parameterized by the mode-map and doubling function. Pattern:

   (defun madolt-insert-show-more-button (mode-map double-fn)
     (magit-insert-section (longer)
       (insert-text-button
        (substitute-command-keys
         (format "Type \\<%s>\\[%s] to show more"
                 mode-map double-fn))
        'action (lambda (_button) (funcall double-fn))
        'follow-link t
        'mouse-face 'magit-section-highlight)))

3. **+ keybinding** in madolt-mode-map (madolt-mode.el):
   (keymap-set madolt-mode-map "+" #'madolt-show-more)
   Where madolt-show-more dispatches based on current buffer mode.

4. **Auto-more hook** (optional, off by default):
   A function on magit-section-movement-hook that auto-expands when cursor lands on a longer section.

## Files affected

- madolt-mode.el (+ keybinding, show-more helper, auto-more hook)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustoms for limits)

## This bead blocks all the per-operation limit beads
close_reason: Implemented show-more infrastructure with 10 tests, all 484 tests pass

title: Constrain diff/log row count with configurable max and expandable [Type + to show more]
description:
P0: Currently a large diff with 100s or even 1000s of rows basically hangs Emacs while it processes all the output. All operations that retrieve rows (diff, log, and any other row-producing operation) should be constrained by default to a configurable maximum number of rows (e.g. 20), with an expand mechanism for the user to fetch more on demand.

## Requirements

1. **Configurable default limit**: A defcustom (e.g. madolt-diff-max-rows, madolt-log-max-rows, or a single madolt-max-rows) that defaults to ~20 rows.

2. **Expandable "show more" mechanism**: When the result is truncated, display a clickable/actionable indicator like `[Type + to show more]` at the truncation point, matching the pattern magit uses in magit-log buffers. Pressing `+` (or clicking) should fetch and display additional rows (another page worth, or all remaining).

3. **Applies to all row-producing operations**: At minimum diff output and log output, but audit all operations that could produce unbounded row counts (blame, conflicts cat, SQL query results, etc.).

## Magit reference

In magit-log, the variable `magit-log-section-commit-count` controls the initial number of commits shown, and `magit-log-double-commit-limit` doubles it when the user presses `+`. The section inserter checks the limit and appends a "Type + to show more" button section when truncated. This is the UX pattern to follow.

## Files likely affected

- madolt-diff.el (row-level diff output)
- madolt-log.el (commit log entries)
- madolt-dolt.el (CLI wrapper -- may need LIMIT/OFFSET support in SQL queries)
- madolt-status.el (inline diffs in status buffer)
- madolt-blame.el (if it produces unbounded output)
- madolt-mode.el (keybinding for `+` to expand)
close_reason: Split into detailed sub-beads per operation

title: Truncate long field values in row-diff summaries to fit terminal width
description:
Row-diff summary lines in the status buffer show all fields as key=value pairs on one line. When fields have long values (e.g. a 'summary' column), the line extends beyond the terminal width and gets truncated by the terminal with a '$' marker, hiding subsequent fields entirely (e.g. 'title' field not visible at all).

Instead, truncate longer field values using the UTF-8 ellipsis character '…' so that as many fields as possible are visible within the available width. There should be a minimum truncated length (e.g. 15 characters) below which values are not truncated further. The algorithm should distribute available space fairly, truncating the longest values first to make room for shorter ones.

This affects madolt-diff--format-row-fields in madolt-diff.el.
close_reason: Implemented truncation of long field values in row-diff summaries. Algorithm iteratively shrinks longest values first with ellipsis to fit within window width. Fixed bug where values at min-width floor still exceeded available space. Added off-by-one fix to avoid terminal continuation indicator. 8 new tests, all 207 pass.

title: Row change summary lines should only truncate at terminal width, not aggressively
description: Lines like '+ id=1  title=First task...' in the status and log views are being truncated too aggressively (the ellipsis appears even when there is plenty of terminal width remaining). They should only be truncated to fit within the terminal/window width.
close_reason: Row-diff sections now start expanded instead of collapsed, eliminating the misleading ellipsis from magit-section-visibility-indicators. Users can still manually collapse with TAB.

title: schema changes in log view should be indented properly
description: Only the first line of the schema changes is currently indented, but every line should be indented by four spaces so that the original indentation structure is preserved.
close_reason: Fixed schema indentation by using replace-regexp-in-string to indent every line of multi-line schema statements, not just the first.

title: Log view should open folded by default
description: When opening the log buffer, commit sections should be collapsed (folded) by default. Currently they may expand to show --stat output immediately, which is noisy. Users should TAB to expand individual commits.
close_reason: Fixed: commit sections in log view now use HIDE=t to start collapsed

title: Inline diff shows (diff not yet available) instead of actual diff
description: Expanding a table section in the status buffer shows '(diff not yet available)' instead of the actual diff content. The fallback in madolt--wash-table-diff is being hit, meaning madolt-diff-insert-table is not found via fboundp. Likely madolt-diff.el is not loaded.
close_reason: Root cause: madolt.el used declare-function instead of require for madolt-apply, madolt-commit, madolt-diff, and madolt-log. Functions were undefined at runtime. Fixed by replacing declare-function with require statements.

title: ? keybinding dispatch menu stopped working
description: Pressing ? in madolt buffers no longer shows the dispatch menu with keybinding help. This was working previously (verified in interactive test madolt-gi5). Something broke it.
close_reason: Root cause: madolt.el used declare-function instead of require for madolt-apply, madolt-commit, madolt-diff, and madolt-log. Functions were undefined at runtime. Fixed by replacing declare-function with require statements.

title: Expanding folded table diff in status buffer jumps to level 4 instead of level 3
description:
Two related bugs in inline diff visibility in the status buffer:

1. TAB/3 on a folded table expanded rows to level 4 (per-field detail) instead of level 3 (one-line summary). Cause: row-diff detail lines were inserted directly, not inside magit-insert-section-body, so there was no heading/body distinction for magit's visibility system to toggle.

2. Pressing + to load more rows inserted new rows at level 4 (fully expanded) instead of matching the current visibility level. Same root cause — without section-body, magit couldn't control the initial visibility of new rows.

Fix: (a) Wrap madolt-diff--insert-row-details in magit-insert-section-body so details become the section body. (b) Set (row-diff . hide) in madolt-status-mode's initial-visibility-alist so rows default to level 3.
close_reason: Wrapped row detail lines in magit-insert-section-body and set row-diff default visibility to hide in status mode. Verified interactively: TAB/3/4/+ all work correctly.

title: Status and log buffers should only show first line of commit message
description:
Commit messages are shown in full (all lines) in status buffer (Head line, recent/unpushed/unpulled commits), log buffer, refs buffer (branches, remotes, tags, cherry commits), and reflog buffer. Only the first line (summary) should be shown. The revision buffer correctly shows the full message.

Fix: add madolt-commit-summary helper in madolt-dolt.el that extracts the first line, and use it at all 10 display points across madolt-status.el, madolt-log.el, madolt-refs.el, and madolt-reflog.el.
close_reason: Added madolt-commit-summary helper; applied at all 10 display points across status, log, refs, and reflog buffers. Revision buffer correctly retains full message.

title: Merge conflict resolution: resolve conflicts then continue merge
description: After DOLT_MERGE detects conflicts, the user should be able to resolve them (via dolt_conflicts tables) and then continue the merge (m m again or similar). Currently conflicts are detected and reported but there is no way to resolve and continue within madolt. Needs: (1) show conflict state in status buffer, (2) provide conflict resolution commands, (3) allow continuing merge after resolution.
close_reason: Implemented full merge conflict resolution workflow: madolt-merge-in-progress-p detection, status header indicator, adaptive merge transient (Continue/Abort), madolt-merge-continue-command, auto-staging after conflict resolve. 6 new tests, all passing.

title: Merge conflict resolution flow: resolve conflicts, then commit
description:
Currently madolt asks for the merge message before the merge runs, so conflicts are detected after the message is already written. The correct magit-like flow for when there are conflicts is: (1) attempt merge, (2) if conflicts, show them and let user resolve via dolt_conflicts tables, (3) user presses m m again to continue the merge, (4) THEN open the merge message buffer, (5) C-c C-c creates the merge commit. This requires splitting the merge into two phases: merge-start and merge-continue.

If there are no conflicts then it should skip steps 2 and 3.
close_reason: Merge now runs immediately on m m without asking for message first. Non-conflict merges use dolt auto-generated message. Conflicts are detected and reported in process buffer. Full conflict resolution flow (resolve then continue) tracked separately.

title: Support --user and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD for fetch/pull/push to HTTP remotes
description: HTTP(S) remotes backed by dolt sql-server require --user and DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD for authentication. Currently madolt has no way to pass these. Need to: (1) add --user as a transient argument for fetch/pull/push, (2) support configuring DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD (possibly per-remote via customize or auth-source). See https://docs.dolthub.com/sql-reference/version-control/remotes#http-s-remotes
close_reason: Added --user transient arg (default root) to fetch/pull/push with auth-source lookup and in-memory session caching for DOLT_REMOTE_PASSWORD. Verified interactively: fetch from d4d railway remote succeeds with --user=root.

title: Fetch silently fails - error not surfaced to user
description: When dolt fetch fails (e.g. auth error), the error is not shown to the user. The fetch appears to complete silently. Need to investigate how fetch errors are reported and fix.
close_reason: Removed SQL translations for fetch/pull/push (DOLT_FETCH/PULL/PUSH return errors as rows with exit 0). Added madolt--clean-output to strip backspace spinner chars from CLI error messages. Fixed 2 pre-existing test failures (database-dir/database-p tests assumed /tmp has no .dolt). Verified interactively in d4d.

title: madolt-commit-assert sees nothing staged when SQL server is enabled
description: When madolt-use-sql-server is t, madolt-commit-assert calls madolt-status-tables outside the refresh cache cycle. This causes madolt--run to route through SQL, returning tab-separated dolt_status rows instead of CLI-formatted output. The CLI parser in madolt-status-tables cannot parse this format, so staged is always empty and 'Nothing staged' is shown even when tables are staged. Fix: make madolt-status-tables handle both CLI and SQL output formats.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt-status-tables now detects SQL output format and parses it correctly

title: Graceful CLI fallback when SQL server connection fails
description: When madolt-connection-ensure or madolt-connection-setup fails to connect to the SQL server (e.g. server died, port conflict, auth failure), it should fall back gracefully to CLI mode rather than erroring or leaving the connection in a broken state that causes subsequent 5-second timeouts per query.
close_reason: Two-tier error reporting: concise user messages in minibuffer, detailed diagnostics in hidden *madolt-sql-log* buffer. Connection validation on connect, graceful CLI fallback on failure.

title: Merge transient should offer completion for merge targets
description:
When invoking a merge (m m), the minibuffer prompt doesn't offer completion of branch/tag names to merge. Should provide completing-read with available branches and tags.

If the point is over a ref / commit-ish, it should default to offering that one
close_reason: Merge now offers completion for local branches, remote branches, and tags via madolt-all-ref-names

title: Refs buffer: text properties should apply to ref name only, not padded width
description: In the refs buffer, face properties (e.g. madolt-branch-local) are applied to the entire fixed-width column including padding spaces after the ref name. They should only apply to the actual ref name text, matching magit's behavior.
close_reason: Fixed in refs buffer commit: visibility level 2, ahead/behind layout, text properties, header space

title: Refs buffer: expand diverged branches at level 3 to show cherry commits
description: When a branch has diverged from its upstream, expanding to level 3 should show individual commits with +/- prefixes (commits ahead/behind), matching magit's cherry commit display in the refs buffer.
close_reason: Expanding diverged branches at level 3 now shows + prefixed commits ahead and - prefixed commits behind upstream

title: Refs buffer: ahead/behind counts should appear before upstream, not after
description: In the refs buffer, the ahead/behind counts (e.g. 24> <9) are displayed after the upstream branch name but should appear before it, matching magit's layout.
close_reason: Fixed in refs buffer commit: visibility level 2, ahead/behind layout, text properties, header space

title: y (show-refs) not showing branches, only tags
description: madolt-show-refs only displays tags, not local or remote branches. The branch data is available (madolt-dolt-lines branch -a works) but the refs buffer isn't rendering the branches section.
close_reason: Fixed: branch-list SQL translation was matching combined flags like -av, causing madolt-branch-list-verbose to get SQL output without hash/message columns

title: refs: add section keymaps for branch/remote/tag actions
description: Magit has per-section-type keymaps: magit-branch-section-map (rename, delete, visit-ref), magit-remote-section-map (rename, remove), magit-tag-section-map (delete, visit-ref). These provide context-sensitive actions on RET and in menus. Madolt has none.
close_reason: Added section keymaps: magit-branch-section-map (RET=checkout, k=delete, R=rename), magit-remote-section-map (k=remove), magit-tag-section-map (k=delete). Only defined if not already set by magit.

title: refs: add transient arguments (--merged, --sort, etc.)
description: Magit's show-refs transient has an Arguments group with --contains, --merged, --no-merged, --sort filter options. Madolt has only the Actions group. Need to check which of these dolt supports and add them.
close_reason: Dolt branch command does not support --merged, --sort, --contains, or --no-merged options. Cannot implement these filter arguments.

title: refs: add cherry commits as expandable sub-sections
description: Magit shows expandable cherry-pick commits (commits on the branch but not on the upstream) as sub-sections under each branch. This is a major feature for comparing branch work. Uses magit-refs--insert-cherry-commits.
close_reason: Added cherry commits as expandable sub-sections under each branch. Uses dolt log upstream..ref to show commits unique to each branch. Content is lazily loaded via magit-insert-section-body.

title: refs: add missing faces (remote-head, upstream, warning)
description: Magit defines magit-branch-remote-head (remote HEAD branch, :box t), magit-branch-upstream (:slant italic), and magit-branch-warning (inherits warning). Madolt has no equivalents. Add madolt-branch-remote-head, madolt-branch-upstream, madolt-branch-warning.
close_reason: Added three missing faces: madolt-branch-remote-head (inherit remote + box), madolt-branch-upstream (inherit remote + italic), madolt-branch-warning (inherit warning)

title: refs: remove colons from section headings (Branches not Branches:)
description: Magit uses 'Branches' and 'Tags' without colons. Madolt uses 'Branches:' and 'Tags:'. Magit-section adds child count automatically (e.g. 'Branches (3)'). Remove the colons to match.
close_reason: Removed colons from section headings: Branches: -> Branches, Tags: -> Tags, Remote X: -> Remote X

title: refs: move comparison target to header-line-format instead of buffer heading
description: Magit puts the comparison target (e.g. HEAD) in the Emacs header-line-format above the buffer content, not as a section heading. Madolt has a visible 'References for HEAD:' magit-section heading in the buffer body. Should use header-line-format like magit and remove the top-level heading. Also change top-level section type from (refs) to (branchbuf) to match magit.
close_reason: Moved comparison target to header-line-format (e.g. 'Comparing with HEAD'). Changed root section type from refs to branchbuf to match magit convention.

title: Implement y => madolt-show-refs like magit
description: Add a 'y' keybinding that opens a refs buffer showing branches and tags, similar to magit-show-refs. This should display local branches, remote branches, and tags in a navigable section-based buffer.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-refs.el with y keybinding, transient, local/remote/tag sections, 18 tests

title: SPC on commit shows revision in other window; repeated SPC/DEL scrolls
description: Pressing SPC on a commit line (in status buffer or log buffer) should open a split window showing the commit revision buffer in the other window. Pressing SPC again while point is still on the same commit should scroll the revision buffer down by one page. DEL (backspace) should scroll it up by one page. This is standard magit behavior (magit-diff-show-or-scroll-up / magit-diff-show-or-scroll-down). Currently SPC has no binding in madolt-mode. Also add a note to AGENTS.md that design choices and keybindings should copy magit wherever possible.
close_reason: Implemented SPC/DEL show-or-scroll, 7 new tests, AGENTS.md design principles section

title: Rebase transient shows continue/abort when no rebase in progress, hides start options during rebase
description: The rebase transient (r) has two bugs: (1) It shows 'continue' and 'abort' options even when no rebase is in progress — these should only appear when a rebase IS active. (2) When a rebase IS in progress, it still shows options to start a new rebase — those should be hidden during an active rebase. Needs :if predicates on the transient suffixes to check for active rebase state, similar to how magit conditionally shows rebase actions.
close_reason: Added :if/:if-not predicates on transient groups, madolt-rebase-in-progress-p detector, 4 new tests

title: Profile and optimize Elisp overhead in staged/unstaged section rendering
description: madolt-insert-staged-changes and madolt-insert-unstaged-changes take 0.3-0.5s each with ZERO CLI calls — pure Elisp overhead. Use elp-instrument-package on madolt-diff to find hot spots. Likely: string formatting, text property application, alist lookups in diff data, or section construction overhead.
close_reason: Two-phase prefetch eliminates all sequential CLI calls during status refresh. 1.86s -> 0.72s (61% faster). All 12 CLI calls now hit cache. Root cause was not pure Elisp overhead but uncached diff/PK/upstream queries.

title: Parallelize independent dolt CLI calls during status refresh
description: Status refresh makes 8 sequential dolt CLI calls (~200ms each = ~1.6s). At least 5 are independent (branch, remote -v, branch -a, status, stash list) and can run in parallel. Demonstrated 4x speedup (1.69s → 0.42s) with bash parallel test. Requires refactoring madolt--run to support async mode via make-process, plus a barrier/callback mechanism for section inserters to wait on their data.
close_reason: Implemented parallel prefetch of 6 independent dolt CLI commands during status refresh. Status header: 0.90s → 0.002s. Stashes: 0.21s → 0.001s. Conflicts: 0.20s → 0.0002s. Total refresh: 2.7s → 1.5s (45% improvement). Also added raw-result caching in madolt--run itself so all callers benefit.

title: Wrap Tab-expand in refresh-cache to eliminate duplicate queries
description: During Tab-expand of sections (commit diff, table diff in log view), madolt--refresh-cache is nil, so identical dolt queries run multiple times. E.g. madolt-primary-key-columns is called per modified row for the same table, each costing ~150ms. Fix: bind madolt--refresh-cache around magit-section-toggle or the washer function. One-line change.
close_reason: Added advice around magit-section-toggle that binds madolt--refresh-cache for madolt buffers. Eliminates duplicate PK queries during Tab-expand (4 CLI calls → 3, 0.773s → 0.669s for commit expand).

title: Profile and report on poor refresh/commit performance
description: Performance in general is still very poor, e.g. refreshing the status buffer or viewing a commit. Do deep profiling to figure out why, and write an .md report suggesting ways forward.
close_reason: Deep profiling complete. Report at docs/performance-report.md. Root cause: dolt CLI startup ~170ms per call × 8 sequential calls = ~1.6s. Key recommendations: (1) parallelize CLI calls (4x speedup demonstrated), (2) persistent sql-server connection, (3) wrap Tab-expand in refresh-cache.

title: Implement j as madolt-status-jump (jump to section in status buffer)
description: The dispatch shows j=Status (madolt-status) but this is wrong — in magit, j is magit-status-jump which jumps to a section within the status buffer (e.g. jump to Staged, Unstaged, Recent commits). Options: (1) Implement madolt-status-jump with a completing-read of section headings, (2) Remove j from the dispatch, (3) Keep j in dispatch only (not mode map) for switching to status from other buffers like log/diff. Currently j is NOT bound in the mode map, only in the dispatch transient.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-status-jump: j bound in mode map, offers completing-read of visible section headings. Dispatch updated from j=Status to j=Jump to section.

title: Reuse existing revision buffers instead of creating new ones
description: When navigating to a revision (e.g. via RET on a commit), if a madolt-revision buffer already exists for that revision, switch to it without refreshing. Currently it may create a new buffer or unnecessarily refresh, which is slow. Should follow magit's pattern of reusing existing buffers.
close_reason: Revision, log, diff (between-commits), blame, and reflog buffers now reuse existing buffers without refreshing. Buffer names include the commit-ish/ref/table for uniqueness.

title: Indent 'Type + to show more' button to match the level of items it refers to
description: The show-more button inserted by madolt-insert-show-more-button is flush-left with no indentation. In inline status diffs, row items are indented with madolt-diff--indent (6 spaces), making the button visually misaligned. The button text should be prefixed with the same indentation as the items above it. Affects all call sites across madolt-diff.el, madolt-log.el, madolt-sql.el, madolt-conflicts.el, madolt-blame.el, madolt-reflog.el.
close_reason: Added optional indent parameter to madolt-insert-show-more-button. Status buffer inline diffs pass madolt-diff--indent (6 spaces) to align the button with row items above it.

title: Fix show-more (+) button not working in status buffer inline diffs
description: madolt-diff-insert-table hardcoded (limit madolt-diff-section-max-rows) ignoring madolt-diff--row-limit. Pressing + doubled the limit variable but the inline renderer never read it. Also madolt-diff-double-limit fell back to madolt-diff-max-rows (100) instead of madolt-diff-section-max-rows (20) in status buffers.
close_reason: Fixed: inline diff now uses (or madolt-diff--row-limit madolt-diff-section-max-rows); double-limit uses section-max-rows fallback in status buffers

title: madolt-status is very slow on large repos
description: madolt-status spawned ~16 dolt subprocesses per refresh (~0.35s each = ~5.6s total). Many were duplicates: branch --show-current called 4x, remote -v 4x, branch -a 3x, log called separately for header (n=1) and recent-commits (n=10). Fix: (1) added magit-style subprocess refresh cache (madolt--refresh-cache) so duplicate calls within one refresh are free, (2) shared log entries between header and recent-commits via madolt--cached-log-entries, (3) added madolt-refresh-verbose toggle (M-x madolt-toggle-verbose-refresh) that logs per-section timing and cache hit/miss stats to *Messages*, matching magit's approach.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add row limit to conflicts cat buffer
description:
The conflicts cat buffer currently renders ALL conflict rows with no limit.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-conflicts-refresh-buffer (madolt-conflicts.el:140) calls madolt--run "conflicts" "cat" table at line 143
2. **CLI command**: dolt conflicts cat <table>
3. **Rendering**: madolt-conflicts--insert-result (madolt-conflicts.el:163) inserts all lines via (dolist (line lines) ...) at line 165
4. **No limit**: All conflict rows are inserted

## Implementation plan

Post-fetch truncation of data lines:

1. Add a defcustom madolt-conflicts-max-rows (default 100)
2. In madolt-conflicts--insert-result, count data lines and stop after N
3. If truncated, insert a show-more button section

## Files affected

- madolt-conflicts.el (truncation + show-more button)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustom)
close_reason: Implemented row limits with show-more buttons. Reflog: 100 lines, Blame: 200 lines, Conflicts: 200 lines. 9 new tests, all pass.

title: Add row limit to blame buffer
description:
The blame buffer currently renders ALL rows from dolt blame with no limit.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-blame-refresh-buffer (madolt-blame.el:83) calls madolt--run "blame" [rev] table
2. **CLI command**: dolt blame [rev] <table> — dolt blame has no native row-limit flag
3. **Rendering**: madolt-blame--insert-result (madolt-blame.el:110) splits output into lines and inserts every line via (dolist (line lines) ...) at line 113
4. **No limit**: All rows from dolt blame output are inserted

## Implementation plan

Since dolt blame has no native limit flag, truncation must be post-fetch:

1. Add a defcustom madolt-blame-max-rows (default 100)
2. In madolt-blame--insert-result, count data lines (non-border, non-header lines) and stop inserting after N
3. If truncated, insert a show-more button section
4. When + is pressed, double the limit and re-render

## Files affected

- madolt-blame.el (truncation + show-more button)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustom)
close_reason: Implemented row limits with show-more buttons. Reflog: 100 lines, Blame: 200 lines, Conflicts: 200 lines. 9 new tests, all pass.

title: Add row limit to reflog buffer
description:
The reflog buffer currently renders ALL reflog entries with no limit.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-reflog-refresh-buffer (madolt-reflog.el:96) calls madolt-reflog-entries (madolt-dolt.el:355-378)
2. **CLI command**: dolt reflog [--all] [ref] — dolt reflog does NOT accept a -n flag, so all entries are returned
3. **Rendering**: madolt-reflog--insert-entry (madolt-reflog.el:115) per entry, iterated at line 111
4. **No limit**: All reflog entries are inserted

## Implementation plan

Since dolt reflog has no native -n flag, truncation must be post-fetch:

1. Add a defcustom madolt-reflog-max-entries (default 100)
2. In madolt-reflog-refresh-buffer, take only the first N entries from the list returned by madolt-reflog-entries
3. If the total count exceeds the limit, insert a show-more button section
4. Bind + in madolt-reflog-mode-map to double the limit and refresh

## Files affected

- madolt-reflog.el (truncation + show-more button)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustom)
close_reason: Implemented row limits with show-more buttons. Reflog: 100 lines, Blame: 200 lines, Conflicts: 200 lines. 9 new tests, all pass.

title: Log view column spacing should minimise truncation and overlap
description:
The spacing between columns in the madolt-log view (hash, author, date, message, refs) is not optimised. This leads to truncation of some fields and overlap of others, especially in narrower windows. The column widths should be calculated dynamically or use better fixed proportions to minimise wasted space and ensure readability.

File: madolt-log.el
close_reason: Fixed right-alignment and column spacing in log margin. Authors padded to fixed width, dates right-aligned within margin width. 2 new tests.

title: Log view times on RHS should be right-aligned
description:
The timestamps displayed on the right-hand side of the madolt-log buffer are not right-aligned. They should be right-aligned to the window edge so that dates line up vertically, matching magit's log display convention.

File: madolt-log.el
close_reason: Fixed right-alignment and column spacing in log margin. Authors padded to fixed width, dates right-aligned within margin width. 2 new tests.

title: Push/pull/fetch transient: remote operations
description:
Add transients for remote operations:
- F: Fetch transient (dolt fetch)
  - F p: fetch from origin
  - F o: fetch from other remote
- P: Push transient (dolt push)
  - P p: push current branch to origin
  - P o: push to other remote
- Pull (could be F f or separate):
  - dolt pull from origin

Backend functions: madolt-fetch, madolt-push, madolt-pull. The madolt-remotes function already exists for remote listing. Should show progress/output in process buffer.
close_reason: Implemented push/pull/fetch transients following magit key conventions (f=fetch, F=pull, P=push). Each transient has origin shortcut and elsewhere option with completing-read. Fetch supports --prune, pull supports --ff-only/--no-ff/--squash, push supports --force/--set-upstream. Error reporting via message area. 23 new tests including integration tests with file:// remotes.

title: Branch transient: create, checkout, delete, rename branches
description:
Add a branch transient (b) with suffixes for common branch operations:
- b b: checkout branch (completing-read from dolt branch)
- b c: create and checkout new branch (dolt checkout -b)
- b n: create branch without checkout (dolt branch <name>)
- b k: delete branch (dolt branch -D, with confirmation)
- b m: rename branch (dolt branch -m)

Backend functions needed: madolt-branch-create, madolt-branch-checkout, madolt-branch-delete, madolt-branch-rename. The completing-read helper madolt--branch-names already exists in madolt-log.el. Add keybinding b in madolt-mode-map.
close_reason: Implemented branch transient (b) with checkout, create & checkout, create (no checkout), delete (with force-delete fallback), and rename. Backend functions in madolt-dolt.el, transient in madolt-branch.el. Moved madolt--branch-names to madolt-dolt.el as madolt-branch-names (with obsolete alias). 19 new tests, all 226 pass. Interactive testing verified all operations.

title: Use magit-consistent colours for row diffs: green added, red deleted, bold column names
description: Row diffs should use the same colour scheme as magit. Added rows should be green, deleted should be red. Within row summaries like '+ id=5  name=Eve', column names (id, name) should be bold, values (5, Eve) should be a different colour, and equals signs should match column name colour but not bold.
close_reason: Row diffs now use magit-consistent colours: added rows green (magit-diff-added), deleted rows red (magit-diff-removed), column names bold, changed cells bold+underline with old=red/new=green, context values dim

title: madolt-log-other always shows current branch log, ignoring selected branch
description: madolt-log-entries in madolt-dolt.el does not accept a branch/rev argument. madolt-log--show stores the rev in madolt-log--rev but madolt-log-refresh-buffer ignores it, always calling (madolt-log-entries limit) without passing the rev. Result: l o <branch> shows the current branch log, not the selected branch.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt-log-entries now accepts rev and extra-args parameters; madolt-log-refresh-buffer passes madolt-log--rev and filtered madolt-log--args through

title: Log transient args (--stat, --merges) are stored but never passed to dolt log
description: madolt-log--args is set from transient-args in madolt-log--show but madolt-log-refresh-buffer ignores it entirely. The -n limit is extracted separately but --stat and --merges have no effect.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt-log-entries now accepts rev and extra-args parameters; madolt-log-refresh-buffer passes madolt-log--rev and filtered madolt-log--args through

title: madolt.el missing require for madolt-status causes empty buffer on startup
description: madolt.el only requires madolt-dolt and madolt-mode, but not madolt-status. When madolt-status is called, it invokes madolt-setup-buffer which calls madolt-refresh, which looks up madolt-status-refresh-buffer by naming convention. Since madolt-status.el was never loaded, the function doesn't exist, so the refresh silently does nothing and the buffer stays empty. Fix: add (require 'madolt-status) to madolt.el.
close_reason: Added (require 'madolt-status) to madolt.el. Status buffer now populates on startup.

title: Write tests for madolt-log.el — commit log viewer
description:
## Overview

Write ERT tests for madolt-log.el in `test/madolt-log-tests.el`.

Depends on: madolt-hb0 (test infrastructure), madolt-3f1.9 (implementation)

## File to create

`test/madolt-log-tests.el`

## Test cases

### Log buffer

1. **test-madolt-log-current-creates-buffer**: Call `madolt-log-current`, verify log buffer is created in `madolt-log-mode`
2. **test-madolt-log-current-shows-commits**: Make several commits, verify all appear in log buffer
3. **test-madolt-log-limit**: Pass `-n 2` argument, verify only 2 commits shown

### Commit sections

4. **test-madolt-log-commit-sections**: Verify each commit is a `magit-section` with type `commit`
5. **test-madolt-log-commit-section-value**: Verify section value is the commit hash string
6. **test-madolt-log-commit-hash-format**: Verify displayed hashes are 8-char abbreviated (or full 32-char base32)
7. **test-madolt-log-commit-shows-message**: Verify commit message text appears in each entry
8. **test-madolt-log-commit-shows-author**: Verify author name appears (in margin or inline)
9. **test-madolt-log-commit-shows-date**: Verify date appears (in margin or inline)

### Navigation

10. **test-madolt-log-ret-on-commit-shows-details**: Position on a commit section, press RET, verify revision buffer opens with diff
11. **test-madolt-log-tab-on-commit-expands-stat**: Position on a commit section, press TAB, verify stat output appears inline

### Transient

12. **test-madolt-log-is-transient**: Verify `madolt-log` is a transient prefix
13. **test-madolt-log-has-current-suffix**: Verify "l" suffix for current branch exists
14. **test-madolt-log-has-other-suffix**: Verify "o" suffix for other branch exists
15. **test-madolt-log-arguments**: Verify documented arguments exist (-n, --stat, --merges, --graph)

### Log margin

16. **test-madolt-log-margin-configurable**: Verify `madolt-log-margin` custom variable exists
17. **test-madolt-log-margin-displays-author-and-date**: Verify margin content includes author and date

### Edge cases

18. **test-madolt-log-single-commit**: Database with only init commit, verify log shows it
19. **test-madolt-log-no-ansi-in-output**: Verify no ANSI escape codes appear in rendered buffer

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All test cases pass in `emacs --batch` mode
- [ ] Tests create multiple commits with distinct messages for verification
- [ ] Section navigation (RET, TAB) is tested programmatically
- [ ] Transient structure verified programmatically
close_reason: Implemented madolt-log.el with log buffer, revision buffer, transient menu, commit stat expansion, branch name completion, parent hash resolution, show-commit. 20 tests passing.

title: Write tests for madolt-diff.el — tabular diff viewer
description:
## Overview

Write ERT tests for madolt-diff.el in `test/madolt-diff-tests.el`.

Depends on: madolt-hb0 (test infrastructure), madolt-3f1.8 (implementation)

## File to create

`test/madolt-diff-tests.el`

## Test cases

### Structured mode (JSON-parsed)

1. **test-madolt-diff-structured-creates-buffer**: Call `madolt-diff-unstaged`, verify diff buffer is created
2. **test-madolt-diff-structured-table-sections**: Modify two tables, verify a `table-diff` section per table
3. **test-madolt-diff-structured-row-added**: Add a row, verify row-diff section with "added" status
4. **test-madolt-diff-structured-row-modified**: Modify a row, verify row-diff section shows old and new values
5. **test-madolt-diff-structured-row-deleted**: Delete a row, verify row-diff section with "deleted" status
6. **test-madolt-diff-structured-cell-highlight**: Modify one cell in a row, verify changed cell is highlighted differently
7. **test-madolt-diff-structured-schema-diff**: ALTER a table schema, verify schema-diff section appears
8. **test-madolt-diff-structured-collapsible-rows**: Verify row-diff sections are collapsible via TAB

### Raw mode (native tabular)

9. **test-madolt-diff-raw-creates-buffer**: Toggle raw mode, verify diff buffer shows tabular output
10. **test-madolt-diff-raw-table-sections**: Verify table blocks are split into sections by `diff --dolt` headers
11. **test-madolt-diff-raw-row-markers-faces**: Verify `<` rows have `madolt-diff-old` face, `>` rows have `madolt-diff-new` face, `+` rows have `madolt-diff-added` face, `-` rows have `madolt-diff-removed` face
12. **test-madolt-diff-raw-header-face**: Verify `diff --dolt` header line has `madolt-diff-table-heading` face

### Diff commands

13. **test-madolt-diff-unstaged-shows-working-changes**: Modify table without staging, verify diff shows changes
14. **test-madolt-diff-staged-shows-staged-changes**: Stage changes, call `madolt-diff-staged`, verify only staged changes shown
15. **test-madolt-diff-staged-empty-when-nothing-staged**: Verify empty/message when nothing staged
16. **test-madolt-diff-commits-between-two**: Make two commits, diff between them, verify changes shown

### Diff stat

17. **test-madolt-diff-stat-shows-counts**: Verify stat output includes row/cell counts

### Inline diff (status buffer integration)

18. **test-madolt-diff-insert-table-at-point**: Call `madolt-diff-insert-table` for a specific table, verify output inserted at point

### Faces

19. **test-madolt-diff-faces-defined**: Verify all documented faces exist: `madolt-diff-added`, `madolt-diff-removed`, `madolt-diff-old`, `madolt-diff-new`, `madolt-diff-context`, `madolt-diff-table-heading`, `madolt-diff-column-header`

### Transient

20. **test-madolt-diff-is-transient**: Verify `madolt-diff` is a transient prefix
21. **test-madolt-diff-has-suffixes**: Verify documented suffixes exist (d, s, c, t)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All test cases pass in `emacs --batch` mode
- [ ] Tests create realistic diff scenarios (added, modified, deleted rows)
- [ ] Face application is verified via `get-text-property` on buffer text
- [ ] Both structured and raw modes are tested
- [ ] Section hierarchy is verified via magit-section API
close_reason: 30 tests covering structured mode, raw mode, row change detection, face propertization, raw line splitting, inline diff, transient, build args, and table name completion. All passing.

title: Write tests for madolt-commit.el — commit transient and message editing
description:
## Overview

Write ERT tests for madolt-commit.el in `test/madolt-commit-tests.el`.

title: Implement test infrastructure — test harness, temp dolt database, Makefile
description:
## Overview

Implement the test infrastructure for madolt. This includes the ERT test harness, a temporary Dolt database macro (analogous to magit's `magit-with-test-repository`), and a Makefile for running tests.

See magit's test infrastructure at `/home/ubuntu/.emacs.d/straight/repos/magit/test/` for reference.

## Files to create

### test/madolt-test-helpers.el

Test helper library providing:

1. **`madolt-with-test-database` macro** — creates a temporary directory, runs `dolt init` in it, sets `default-directory`, runs body, then cleans up:
   ```elisp
   (defmacro madolt-with-test-database (&rest body)
     "Execute BODY in a temporary Dolt database directory.
   The database is initialized with `dolt init` and cleaned up afterward."
     (declare (indent 0) (debug t))
     `(let ((dir (file-name-as-directory (make-temp-file "madolt-test-" t))))
        (condition-case err
            (let ((default-directory (file-truename dir))
                  (process-environment
                   (cons "NO_COLOR=1" process-environment)))
              (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil "init")
              (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil
                            "config" "--local" "--add" "user.name" "Test User")
              (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil
                            "config" "--local" "--add" "user.email" "test@example.com")
              ,@body)
          (error (message "Keeping test directory:\n  %s" dir)
                 (signal (car err) (cdr err))))
        (delete-directory dir t)))
   ```

2. **`madolt-test-create-table` helper** — creates a table via `dolt sql`:
   ```elisp
   (defun madolt-test-create-table (name columns-sql)
     "Create table NAME with COLUMNS-SQL in the current test database."
     (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil
                   "sql" "-q" (format "CREATE TABLE %s (%s)" name columns-sql)))
   ```

3. **`madolt-test-insert-row` helper** — inserts a row:
   ```elisp
   (defun madolt-test-insert-row (table values-sql)
     "Insert a row into TABLE with VALUES-SQL."
     (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil
                   "sql" "-q" (format "INSERT INTO %s VALUES %s" table values-sql)))
   ```

4. **`madolt-test-commit` helper** — stages all and commits:
   ```elisp
   (defun madolt-test-commit (message)
     "Stage all tables and commit with MESSAGE."
     (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil "add" ".")
     (call-process madolt-dolt-executable nil nil nil "commit" "-m" message))
   ```

5. **`madolt-test-setup-populated-db` helper** — creates a database with known state for common test scenarios (a committed table, a staged change, an unstaged change, and an untracked table).

### Makefile

```makefile
EMACS ?= emacs
LOAD_PATH = -L . -L test

.PHONY: test compile clean

test: compile
	$(EMACS) --batch $(LOAD_PATH) \
	  -l test/madolt-test-helpers.el \
	  -l test/madolt-dolt-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-process-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-mode-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-status-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-apply-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-commit-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-diff-tests.el \
	  -l test/madolt-log-tests.el \
	  -f ert-run-tests-batch-and-exit

compile:
	$(EMACS) --batch $(LOAD_PATH) \
	  --eval "(setq byte-compile-error-on-warn t)" \
	  -f batch-byte-compile madolt-dolt.el madolt-process.el \
	  madolt-mode.el madolt.el madolt-status.el madolt-apply.el \
	  madolt-commit.el madolt-diff.el madolt-log.el

clean:
	rm -f *.elc test/*.elc
```

Also add a `test-file` target for running individual test files:
```makefile
test-%: compile
	$(EMACS) --batch $(LOAD_PATH) \
	  -l test/madolt-test-helpers.el \
	  -l test/$@s.el \
	  -f ert-run-tests-batch-and-exit
```

## Dependencies

- None (this is foundational test infrastructure)
- But it requires `dolt` to be available in PATH

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `madolt-with-test-database` macro creates a temp dir, runs `dolt init`, and cleans up
- [ ] Helper functions (create-table, insert-row, commit) work correctly
- [ ] `madolt-test-setup-populated-db` produces a database with staged, unstaged, and untracked changes
- [ ] Makefile `test` target runs all test files via ERT batch mode
- [ ] Makefile `compile` target byte-compiles all source files with warnings-as-errors
- [ ] Test infrastructure does not depend on any madolt source files (only on dolt binary)
- [ ] File provides 'madolt-test-helpers feature
close_reason: Implemented and all 45 tests passing

title: Implement madolt-log.el — commit log viewer
description:
## Overview

Implement madolt-log.el (~250 LOC), the commit log viewer. Displays commit history in a navigable, section-based buffer. Each commit is a collapsible section; RET shows full commit details with diff.

See specs/MVP.md section 'madolt-log.el (~250 LOC)' for full design.

Reference implementation: /home/ubuntu/.emacs.d/straight/repos/magit/lisp/magit-log.el

## File to create

`madolt-log.el`

## Dependencies

- `madolt-dolt` (for `madolt-log-entries`, `madolt-dolt-insert`, `madolt-current-branch`)
- `madolt-mode` (for `madolt-log-mode`, `madolt-setup-buffer`)
- `madolt-diff` (for rendering commit diffs in revision buffers)
- `magit-section` (for section insertion)
- `transient` (for log transient menu)

## What to implement

### 1. Transient menu

```elisp
(transient-define-prefix madolt-log-transient ()
  "Show log."
  ["Arguments"
   ("-n" "Limit count" "-n"
    :class transient-option
    :reader transient-read-number-N+)
   ("-s" "Show stat"   "--stat")
   ("-m" "Merges only" "--merges")
   ("-g" "Graph"       "--graph")]
  ["Log"
   ("l" "Current branch" madolt-log-current)
   ("o" "Other branch"   madolt-log-other)
   ("h" "HEAD"           madolt-log-head)])
```

### 2. Log commands

```elisp
(defun madolt-log-current (&optional args)
  "Show log for the current branch."
  (interactive (list (transient-args 'madolt-log-transient)))
  (madolt-log--show (madolt-current-branch) args))

(defun madolt-log-other (branch &optional args)
  "Show log for BRANCH."
  (interactive
   (list (completing-read "Branch: " (madolt--branch-names))
         (transient-args 'madolt-log-transient)))
  (madolt-log--show branch args))

(defun madolt-log-head (&optional args)
  "Show log for HEAD."
  (interactive (list (transient-args 'madolt-log-transient)))
  (madolt-log--show "HEAD" args))
```

### 3. Log display

```elisp
(defun madolt-log--show (rev args)
  "Show log for REV with ARGS in a log buffer.")
```

Implementation:
1. Store REV and ARGS as buffer-local variables
2. Call `(madolt-setup-buffer 'madolt-log-mode)`

```elisp
(defun madolt-log-refresh-buffer ()
  "Refresh the log buffer.")
```

Implementation:
1. Get commits via `(madolt-log-entries N)` where N comes from the -n arg (default 25)
2. Insert a root section of type `log`
3. Heading: `Commits on BRANCH:`
4. For each commit, insert a child section of type `commit` with value = hash:
   - Heading format: `SHORT-HASH  DATE  AUTHOR  MESSAGE`
   - SHORT-HASH = first 8 chars, displayed in `madolt-hash` face (dim)
   - DATE = formatted date, displayed in date face
   - AUTHOR = author name
   - MESSAGE = first line of commit message
   - Refs (if present): shown inline after hash, e.g., `(HEAD -> main)` in ref face

### 4. Log margin

```elisp
(defcustom madolt-log-margin '(t "%Y-%m-%d" 16 nil nil)
  "Format of the margin in log buffers.
Value is a list (INIT STYLE WIDTH AUTHOR AUTHOR-WIDTH).
INIT is whether to show the margin initially.
STYLE is the date format string.
WIDTH is the margin width.
AUTHOR is whether to show author.
AUTHOR-WIDTH is the author column width.")
```

Display author and date in a right-aligned margin area. Use `(setq-local left-margin-width ...)` or overlay-based margin rendering.

For MVP, a simpler approach is to align author+date in the main buffer using fixed-width columns rather than actual Emacs margins. This is what the heading format already does.

### 5. Commit section expansion

When TAB is pressed on a commit section, show --stat output inline:

```elisp
(magit-insert-section (commit hash nil
                       :washer #'madolt-log--wash-commit)
  (magit-insert-heading formatted-line))

(defun madolt-log--wash-commit ()
  "Insert stat output for the commit at point."
  (let ((hash (oref (magit-current-section) value)))
    ;; Run dolt diff HASH^ HASH --stat and insert output
    ;; Or run dolt show HASH --stat
    ))
```

### 6. Show commit (RET)

```elisp
(defun madolt-show-commit (hash)
  "Show commit HASH in a revision buffer."
  (interactive
   (list (oref (magit-current-section) value)))
  ...)
```

Implementation:
1. Create a new buffer in `madolt-revision-mode` (derive from `madolt-diff-mode`)
2. Show commit metadata (hash, author, date, message) at the top
3. Show the full diff below using `madolt-diff` rendering functions

The diff is generated by `dolt diff PARENT_HASH HASH`. To get the parent hash, use `dolt log --oneline -n 1 --parents HASH` or query `dolt_commit_ancestors`.

Alternatively, `dolt show HASH` outputs both commit info and diff together, which can be parsed.

### 7. Revision mode

```elisp
(define-derived-mode madolt-revision-mode madolt-diff-mode "Madolt Rev"
  "Mode for showing a single commit's details and diff.")

(defun madolt-revision-refresh-buffer ()
  "Refresh the revision buffer.")
```

The revision buffer layout:
```
commit a4i9j85edq6ke1kgcqaeodlbv93knbm5 (HEAD -> main)
Author: Alice <alice@example.com>
Date:   Sat Mar 07 12:18:45 +0000 2026

    Add users table with initial data

diff --dolt a/users b/users
  [table-diff sections as in madolt-diff.el]
```

### 8. Helper functions

```elisp
(defun madolt--branch-names ()
  "Return a list of branch name strings."
  ;; Parse dolt branch output, strip '* ' prefix from current branch
  )
```

### 9. Faces

```elisp
(defface madolt-log-author
  '((t :foreground "cyan"))
  "Face for author names in log.")

(defface madolt-log-date
  '((t :foreground "green"))
  "Face for dates in log.")
```

## Buffer-local variables

```elisp
(defvar-local madolt-log-rev nil "Revision being logged.")
(defvar-local madolt-log-args nil "Current log arguments.")
(defvar-local madolt-revision-hash nil "Hash of commit being shown.")
```

## Testing approach

1. Using the test data from previous beads (should have commits)
2. Test `l l` — should show log for current branch
3. Verify each commit is a collapsible section
4. Press TAB on a commit — should show stat output
5. Press RET on a commit — should open revision buffer with full diff
6. Test `l o` with a branch name
7. Test -n limit argument (change to 5, verify only 5 shown)
8. Test --stat argument (inline stats for all commits)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Transient menu with log arguments and branch selection
- [ ] `l l` shows log for current branch
- [ ] `l o` prompts for branch with completion
- [ ] `l h` shows log for HEAD
- [ ] Each commit is a section with hash, date, author, message
- [ ] Short hash displayed with dim face
- [ ] Refs displayed inline when present
- [ ] TAB on commit shows stat output inline
- [ ] RET on commit opens revision buffer with full diff
- [ ] Revision buffer shows commit metadata + diff
- [ ] -n argument limits number of commits shown
- [ ] File provides 'madolt-log feature

Tests must pass before this bead can be considered fully complete.
