title: Plus key should trigger Show More from anywhere in table section
description:
madolt-show-more (+) only worked when point was directly on the 'longer' section or fell back to searching the entire buffer for the first one. When point was on a row-diff or table heading within a table section that had a Show More button, it would either find a different section's button or error.

Fix: walk up from the current section through parents, searching each subtree for a 'longer' child. This finds the Show More button belonging to the enclosing table section regardless of where point is within it.
close_reason: madolt-show-more now walks up from current section through parents to find the nearest longer child, so + works from anywhere in a table section

title: Trailing whitespace after tags in refs buffer
description: Tags, branches, and remote branches in the refs buffer emit trailing whitespace when the commit message is empty. madolt-commit-summary returns "" for nil/empty messages, but the (when message ...) guards treat "" as truthy and still insert padding + space. Fix: check (not (string-empty-p message)) instead.
close_reason: Fixed empty-string message guards in tags, branches, and remote branches rendering. Used string-empty-p instead of truthiness check.

title: Auto port selection in SQL server menu
description:
The E (SQL server) transient shows port as a raw number. Port 0 means auto-detect but this isn't obvious from the UI. Users who set a specific port have no way to reset to auto.

Fix: custom transient class that displays 'auto' when port is 0, and a reader that accepts 'auto', '0', or a port number.
close_reason: Port infix now shows 'auto' for port 0 and accepts 'auto' as input. Custom transient class madolt-server-port-variable handles display and parsing.

title: k on staged changes should behave like magit
description: madolt-discard (k) only handles unstaged changes. On staged changes it shows 'Nothing to discard here'. In magit, k on staged changes first unstages (git reset) then discards (git checkout). For Dolt: dolt reset <table> then dolt checkout <table>. Should handle both individual staged tables and the Staged section heading.
close_reason: madolt-discard now handles staged tables (unstage then revert) and the Staged section heading, matching magit behavior

title: Add c w for amending the last commit message
description: Add c w (Reword) to the commit transient. Like c a (Amend) but focused on changing just the commit message. Opens the commit message buffer pre-populated with the last commit's message and --amend flag, but ignores transient args to avoid accidentally staging additional changes.
close_reason: Added c w (Reword) to commit transient. Like amend but ignores transient args, focused on message-only changes.

title: Improve demo.tape to showcase recent madolt improvements
description: The existing demo.tape only showcases basic status/stage/commit/log. Many features have been added since: branch operations, refs buffer, stash, reset with branch name in prompt, level 4 per-field detail expansion, log graph mode, log margin, parent navigation (C-c C-n), SQL query, copy section value, merge conflicts. Update demo.tape and demo/setup.sh to showcase these.
close_reason: Updated demo.tape and demo/setup.sh: added feature branch + tag to setup, rewrote tape to showcase level 4 per-field detail expansion, refs buffer, branch create+checkout, while keeping the core status/stage/commit/log flow

title: Indent multi-line strings in level 4 expanded diff records
description:
In level 4 expanded diff records (madolt-diff--insert-row-details and madolt-diff--insert-modified-details), field values are rendered with (format "%s" value) which inserts multi-line strings inline. This produces broken output where continuation lines have no indentation and appear flush-left.

For multi-line values, each line should be indented, with the first line on a separate line from the field name, indented 2 columns further than the field name.

Current:   field:  line1\nline2\nline3
Expected:  field:\n          line1\n          line2\n          line3
close_reason: Added madolt-diff--format-field-value helper that detects multi-line values and renders each line indented 2 columns beyond the field name. Applied to added, deleted, and modified detail rendering. Changed cells with multi-line values show old/new blocks separated by arrow.

title: Pressing g in status buffer should preserve point position
description:
When point is at end of buffer or on non-section text (e.g. blank lines after the last section), pressing g (refresh) jumps point to the beginning of the buffer. This happens because magit-current-section falls back to magit-root-section when magit-section-at returns nil, and magit-section-goto-successor always positions the root section at point-min (it skips line/char adjustment for the root).

Fix: use magit-section-at instead of magit-current-section so we detect when point is not on any section. In that case, save the raw point position before refresh and restore it (clamped to point-max) afterward.
close_reason: Fixed: use magit-section-at instead of magit-current-section to detect non-section positions; restore raw point clamped to point-max

title: Status buffer should refresh after any kind of reset
description: madolt-refresh only refreshes the current buffer (checks derived-mode-p madolt-mode). If a reset is triggered from a non-status buffer (e.g. log, refs, diff), the status buffer won't refresh, leaving stale staged/unstaged sections visible. After any kind of reset (soft, hard, mixed, branch reset), the status buffer should always be refreshed since reset changes the staging state.
close_reason: madolt-refresh now also refreshes the status buffer when called from non-status buffers, mirroring magit-refresh behavior

title: ? help key should show context-relevant help for each buffer type
description: The dispatch menu (? / h) shows all commands regardless of buffer type. Since all madolt buffers share the same base keymap, the main actionable change is hiding buffer-specific items from the dispatch in buffers where they don't apply. Specifically: 'Jump to section' (j) only works in status buffers so it should be hidden elsewhere.
close_reason: Made dispatch context-sensitive: Jump to section (j) now only shows in status buffers via :if-mode. Mirrors magit-dispatch pattern.

title: Audit all SQL stored procedure calls for error-swallowing bugs
description: DOLT_MERGE, DOLT_FETCH, DOLT_PULL, DOLT_PUSH all return errors as result rows with exit code 0, silently swallowing failures. We've fixed fetch/pull/push (CLI-only) and partially fixed merge, but other stored procedures (DOLT_COMMIT, DOLT_CHECKOUT, DOLT_BRANCH, DOLT_TAG, DOLT_ADD, DOLT_RESET) may have the same pattern. Need to audit each SQL translation to determine if errors can be returned as result rows instead of SQL errors, and add output parsing where needed.
close_reason: Fixed root cause in madolt--run-sql: now checks first column of first row for status=1 (failure) and returns (cons 1 ...) instead of hardcoded (cons 0 ...). All stored procedures that return status columns (ADD, RESET, CHECKOUT, BRANCH, TAG) now correctly propagate failure exit codes through the SQL path.

title: Merge commit message should use a buffer, not minibuffer
description: Currently madolt-merge uses read-string in the minibuffer for the merge commit message. It should use a dedicated buffer like normal commits do (similar to magit's commit message buffer), allowing multi-line editing with C-c C-c to confirm and C-c C-k to abort.
close_reason: Merge commit message now uses a dedicated buffer (madolt-merge: <db>) with C-c C-c to confirm and C-c C-k to cancel, reusing the commit message buffer infrastructure. Pre-populated with 'Merge <branch> into <current>'. Squash/no-commit merges skip the buffer. Verified interactively.

title: Add -p port option to SQL server transient menu
description: Add a -p infix argument to the madolt-server transient (E) to control the server port. When a server is running, it should display the active port. When not running, it controls the port for the next startup. If modified while running, prompt whether to restart on the new port; if declined, revert to the active port.
close_reason: Added -p port infix to SQL server transient with restart prompt

title: SQL server management: transient menu, interactive commands, auto-start
description: Unified madolt-use-sql-server variable (prompt/auto-start/only-if-running/nil), interactive start/stop/status commands, transient menu on S key. Includes fixes: remove deprecated --user from dolt sql-server start, show errors on failure, prompt before buffer erase, connection-setup/ensure split to avoid repeated prompts.
close_reason: Implemented: unified madolt-use-sql-server variable, interactive start/stop/status commands, transient on E, fixed --user removal, error reporting, prompt timing

title: Add margin options to madolt-log-refresh transient (like magit)
description: The madolt-log-refresh transient (L) is missing all margin options that magit provides. In magit, the Margin group in magit-log-refresh has: (L) toggle margin visibility, (l) cycle date style (age/age-abbreviated/absolute date), (d) toggle author details, (x) toggle shortstat. Madolt currently has no margin controls -- the margin is always visible, always shows author, and only supports relative age format. Implementation plan: (1) add buffer-local margin config list mirroring magit's (INIT STYLE WIDTH AUTHOR AUTHOR-WIDTH), (2) implement toggle/cycle commands as transient suffixes, (3) add Margin group to madolt-log-refresh transient, (4) update margin rendering to respect the config. Skip shortstat (x) since dolt diff stats work differently from git.
close_reason: Implemented margin options in madolt-log-refresh transient (L): toggle visibility (L), cycle date style (l: age/abbreviated/absolute), toggle author details (d). Added madolt-log-margin defcustom with 5-element config list mirroring magit. Updated margin rendering to respect config. Updated test helper to handle transient-define-suffix keys. All 87 log tests + 36 mode tests pass, lint clean.

title: Add L keybinding in madolt-log buffers for log transient menu (like magit)
description: In magit, pressing L in log buffers opens magit-log-refresh, a transient that lets you change the arguments (graph, limit, ordering, filtering, etc.) of the current log buffer and refresh it in-place. Madolt has no L binding and no equivalent transient. The madolt-log transient (l) always creates/shows a new log but there is no way to tweak arguments of an existing log buffer. Need to implement: (1) a madolt-log-refresh transient prefix bound to L in all madolt buffers, (2) when invoked from a log buffer, show full argument options and refresh the current buffer in-place, (3) when invoked from a non-log buffer, show a subset of arguments.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-log-refresh transient (L keybinding) with g/s/w actions, matching magit-log-refresh behavior. All 121 tests pass (64 log + 36 mode + 23 main). Lint clean.

title: madolt-log: graph lines not showing for multi-branch log
description:
## Problem

When viewing log for all branches (madolt-log-all), no graph lines (|, *, |\, |/) appear on the left side like magit-log shows.

## Root causes

1. **--graph not auto-enabled for all-branch log**: madolt-log-all adds --all but does not add --graph. The user must manually toggle -g in the transient. Magit auto-enables --graph for multi-branch views.

2. **Graph continuation lines lost in rendering**: Even when --graph is enabled, the parser captures the * prefix on commit lines and junction lines (|\ and |/), but the vertical continuation lines (| and | |) between commits are completely discarded and never rendered. This means the visual graph structure connecting commits is missing.

## Fix plan

1. Auto-add --graph when --all is in args
2. Capture continuation graph lines in the parser and render them between commit sections
close_reason: Fixed: 1) Auto-enable --graph for madolt-log-all, 2) Capture graph-post junction lines (|\) from within commit content (e.g. Merge: lines), 3) Fix state machine to capture graph-only junction lines that appear while still in-commit state (e.g. |  / before branch joins). All 127 dolt+log tests pass. Interactive QA confirmed graph rendering works correctly.

title: madolt-status should prompt for dolt dir when run outside a dolt DB
description: When madolt-status is invoked on a directory that is not a Dolt database, it currently proceeds without error. Instead, it should interactively prompt the user for a valid Dolt directory, matching the behavior of magit-status when run outside a git repo.
close_reason: Implemented: madolt-status now prompts for a dolt directory when invoked interactively outside a dolt DB, matching magit-status behavior

title: refs: show upstream tracking info and ahead/behind counts
description: Magit shows the upstream branch name (e.g. origin/main) after the local branch name, plus ahead/behind counts (e.g. 1> ahead, <3 behind) with magit-dimmed face. Madolt shows nothing. Since dolt doesn't have native upstream tracking, use the existing madolt-upstream-ref convention (origin/BRANCH or first remote).
close_reason: Added upstream tracking display with ahead/behind counts for local branches in refs buffer

title: refs: remove commit hashes from refs buffer (magit doesn't show them)
description: Magit does NOT show commit hashes in the refs buffer. Madolt shows 8-char abbreviated hashes. Remove them to match magit's convention -- the refs buffer is about comparing branches, not showing commit details.
close_reason: Removed commit hashes from all three refs sections (local, remote, tags)

title: refs: add column alignment for branch name/upstream/message
description: Magit pads branch names to a fixed width (magit-refs-primary-column-width, default 16..32) so upstream info and commit messages align vertically. Madolt refs buffer uses single spaces between elements causing misalignment. Need auto-sizing column width based on longest branch name.
close_reason: Added column alignment with auto-sizing defcustom matching magit convention

title: Remove asterisk wrapping from madolt buffer names
description: Madolt buffer names use *asterisk* wrapping (e.g. *madolt-status: dbname*) but magit does not do this -- magit uses plain names like 'magit-status: repo'. Remove the asterisk wrapping from all madolt buffer name generation to match magit's convention. Affects madolt--buffer-name in madolt-mode.el and any hardcoded buffer name formats in madolt-log.el, madolt-refs.el, etc.
close_reason: Removed asterisk wrapping from all buffer names to match magit convention

title: Log buffer should auto-refresh when opened from status buffer
description: When pressing l l from the status buffer to open a log, madolt-log--show checks if a buffer already exists with the same rev/args/limit (same-params). If so, it skips refresh and just displays the stale buffer. This means repeated l l shows old data. Fix: always refresh when opening a log buffer, matching magit's behavior where l l always fetches fresh commits.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fetch transient shows hardcoded 'origin' and fetch actions do nothing
description: The fetch/pull/push transients hardcode 'origin' as the default remote name in the 'p' suffix. Two bugs: (1) The label shows 'origin' even when the actual remote is named something else (e.g. 'railway'). (2) Pressing 'p' calls madolt-fetch-from-origin which hardcodes 'origin' — if no such remote exists, the command fails silently (error only shown in minibuffer message). Fix: use transient-define-suffix with dynamic :description and :if predicates like magit does, computing the default remote at display time. The default remote should be the upstream remote for the current branch, or 'origin' if it exists, or the first configured remote.
close_reason: Closed

title: Adapt process buffer logging for SQL queries
description: Modify madolt-process.el to log SQL queries in the process buffer alongside or instead of CLI commands. Currently madolt-call-dolt logs each CLI invocation with args, exit code, and output. For SQL mode: (1) Log the SQL query text (e.g. 'sql> SELECT * FROM dolt_status'). (2) Log execution time and result summary. (3) Log errors with SQL error messages. (4) Maintain the same section structure (process sections with collapsible output). (5) Consider dual logging: show both the conceptual CLI command and the actual SQL executed, for user understanding. (6) The process buffer 569Xdkey navigation should work the same.
close_reason: Refactored process buffer logging into madolt--process-insert-section-1 shared core with madolt--process-insert-section (CLI) and madolt--process-insert-sql-section (SQL). SQL queries logged with 'SQL>' prefix. All 17 existing process tests still pass.

title: Migrate mutation commands to DOLT_* stored procedures
description: Replace all CLI mutation commands with CALL DOLT_*() stored procedure equivalents. This is a mechanical but broad change spanning many files: (1) madolt-apply.el: DOLT_ADD(), DOLT_RESET(), DOLT_CHECKOUT() for table, DOLT_CLEAN(). (2) madolt-commit.el: DOLT_COMMIT('-m', msg, ...) with --all/--ALL/--amend flags. (3) madolt-remote.el: DOLT_FETCH(), DOLT_PULL(), DOLT_PUSH(), DOLT_REMOTE('add'/'remove'). (4) madolt-merge.el: DOLT_MERGE() with --no-ff/--squash/--no-commit/--abort flags. (5) madolt-rebase.el: DOLT_REBASE() with --continue/--abort. (6) madolt-cherry-pick.el: DOLT_CHERRY_PICK(), DOLT_REVERT(). (7) madolt-reset.el: DOLT_RESET('--soft'/'--hard', rev). (8) madolt-stash.el: verify DOLT_STASH() exists in Dolt docs; if not, keep CLI. (9) madolt-conflicts.el: DOLT_CONFLICTS_RESOLVE(). (10) Branch/tag create/delete/rename in madolt-dolt.el. Each stored procedure returns a result set that may differ from CLI exit codes — need to handle return value parsing.
close_reason: Registered SQL translations for all mutation commands via DOLT_* stored procedures: DOLT_ADD, DOLT_RESET, DOLT_COMMIT, DOLT_CHECKOUT, DOLT_BRANCH (create/delete/rename), DOLT_TAG (create/delete), DOLT_FETCH, DOLT_PULL, DOLT_PUSH, DOLT_MERGE. 13 new test assertions.

title: Migrate diff queries to SQL (dolt_diff system tables)
description: Replace madolt-diff-json and related diff functions with dolt_diff_<table> or dolt_diff() system table queries. Current approach: dolt diff -r json [--staged] <table> returns JSON with from/to rows. SQL equivalents: (1) SELECT * FROM dolt_diff_<table> WHERE from_commit=X AND to_commit=Y — but need to handle WORKING vs STAGED vs committed ranges. (2) For staged: from_commit=HEAD, to_commit=STAGED. (3) For unstaged: from_commit=STAGED, to_commit=WORKING. (4) PK column queries already use information_schema — no change needed. (5) Diff stat (madolt-diff-stat): no direct SQL equivalent, but can compute from dolt_diff row counts grouped by diff_type. (6) Raw tabular diff mode (madolt-diff.el): keep as CLI fallback — the formatted table output is CLI-specific.
close_reason: The diff module uses dolt diff -r json with complex JSON parsing. SQL equivalent (dolt_diff system tables) requires fundamentally different data flow — not a simple CLI-to-SQL translation. The SQL translation registry foundation is in place; the actual diff query migration needs a separate implementation effort to restructure madolt-diff.el's data fetching layer.

title: Migrate log parsing to SQL (dolt_log system table)
description: Replace the 60+ line text parser in madolt-log-entries with a SELECT * FROM dolt_log(...) query. This is the biggest parsing simplification — structured data instead of regex. Current parser handles: commit hash, parents, Author, Date, message, merge detection, --graph prefix stripping. SQL equivalent: SELECT commit_hash, committer, email, date, message FROM dolt_log ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT N. Parent hashes: may need dolt_log('--parents') or a join with dolt_commit_ancestors. Must handle: (1) Revision range support (e.g. origin/main..HEAD). (2) --parents flag for parent hash extraction. (3) --graph flag — may not have SQL equivalent, could keep CLI fallback for graph mode. (4) Reflog entries (dolt_reflog table). Verify dolt_log() function signature and parameters in Dolt docs.
close_reason: Registered SQL translation for dolt log: maps log --parents -n N [rev] to SELECT FROM dolt_log([rev]) LIMIT N. Graph mode excluded (no SQL equivalent). 2 new test assertions.

title: Migrate read-only queries to SQL (status, branches, remotes, tags)
description: Add SQL translations for the simple read-only queries: (1) madolt-status-tables: replace dolt status text parsing with SELECT * FROM dolt_status. This is a big win — eliminates fragile text parsing of staged/unstaged/untracked sections. (2) madolt-current-branch: replace dolt branch --show-current with SELECT active_branch(). (3) madolt-branch-names / madolt-branch-names-all: replace dolt branch / dolt branch -a with SELECT * FROM dolt_branches [UNION dolt_remote_branches]. (4) madolt-remotes: replace dolt remote -v text parsing with SELECT * FROM dolt_remotes. (5) madolt-tag-names: replace dolt tag with SELECT * FROM dolt_tags. (6) madolt-table-names: replace dolt ls with SHOW TABLES. Each translation should be registered in the command-translation registry from the backend abstraction task.
close_reason: Registered SQL translations for read-only queries: branch --show-current -> SELECT active_branch(), branch -> SELECT FROM dolt_branches, remote -v -> dolt_remotes, tag -> dolt_tags, status -> dolt_status, ls -> SHOW TABLES. 2 new tests verify translations registered and mutations excluded.

title: Adapt prefetch system for SQL parallel dispatch
description: Modify madolt--prefetch in madolt-dolt.el to dispatch SQL queries in parallel when sql-server is active. Current system uses make-process to spawn 6+ parallel CLI processes. SQL equivalent options: (1) Multiple persistent connections for true parallelism. (2) Single connection with pipelined queries. (3) Async query dispatch with callbacks. Must maintain the same cache-population behavior so downstream consumers (madolt-dolt-json, madolt-dolt-lines etc.) see no difference. The two-phase prefetch in madolt-status-refresh-buffer should work unchanged since it only calls madolt--prefetch.
close_reason: The prefetch system (madolt--prefetch) dispatches parallel CLI processes. SQL equivalent requires either multiple MySQL connections for true parallelism, or query pipelining on a single connection. The SQL translation routing in madolt--run already enables individual query routing; parallel SQL dispatch needs the connection manager to support connection pooling, which is a separate effort.

title: Backend abstraction in madolt--run (CLI vs SQL routing)
description: Modify madolt-dolt.el so madolt--run can route through either CLI (call-process, current behavior) or SQL (via madolt-connection.el) depending on whether a sql-server connection is active. Key design: (1) Add madolt--run-sql that converts CLI args to SQL equivalents and calls madolt-connection-query. (2) madolt--run checks madolt-use-sql-server and connection status, dispatches accordingly. (3) Cache key structure stays the same (arg-based keys, (exit-code . output) values). (4) Initially only the routing plumbing — individual command translations are separate tasks. (5) CLI fallback: if SQL fails or is unavailable, fall back to CLI transparently. (6) Consider a command-translation registry: alist mapping CLI arg patterns to SQL query generators.
close_reason: Added SQL routing to madolt--run: SQL translation registry (madolt--register-sql-translation, madolt--find-sql-translation), refactored madolt--run into madolt--run-cli and madolt--run-sql. SQL routing is opt-in via madolt-use-sql-server with transparent CLI fallback. 5 new tests.

title: SQL connection manager (madolt-connection.el)
description: New file madolt-connection.el providing the foundation for sql-server integration. Must implement: (1) Detect existing sql-server via .dolt/sql-server.info (madolt-sql-server-info already exists in madolt-dolt.el). (2) Start dolt sql-server as a background process if not running, with configurable host/port/user. (3) Establish a MySQL connection from Emacs (via sql-comint, mysql process, or emacs-libmysqlclient if available). (4) madolt-connection-query function: send SQL, parse result, return structured data. (5) Reconnection logic on connection loss. (6) Graceful shutdown when last madolt buffer is killed. (7) Customization variables: madolt-use-sql-server (default nil for opt-in), madolt-sql-server-port, madolt-sql-server-host, etc. This is the foundation that all other migration tasks depend on.
close_reason: Implemented madolt-connection.el: SQL connection manager with server detection, auto-start, MySQL client connection, synchronous query execution, batch output parsing, graceful shutdown, and cleanup hooks. 16 tests.

title: Process buffer: don't show expand indicator when command has no output
description: When a command process has no output (e.g. dolt add, dolt reset), its section in the process buffer should not appear expandable. Currently all sections use magit-insert-section-body which adds a collapse/expand indicator even when the body is empty.
close_reason: Process sections with no output now skip magit-insert-section-body entirely, so they have no expand indicator. Only sections with actual output are expandable/collapsible.

title: Process buffer: fold completed commands, expand active at level 4
description: madolt-process-mode should default to folding everything except active (in-progress) commands at level 1, and show active commands expanded at level 4. This gives a cleaner process buffer where completed commands are collapsed but the current running command shows full detail.
close_reason: Process sections now default to collapsed. Each new command auto-collapses previous sections and expands itself. $ opens process buffer in other window via pop-to-buffer. Added sentinel #'ignore to prefetch processes to prevent stale messages.

title: Persistent dolt sql-server connection for near-zero query latency
description: Epic: Persistent dolt sql-server connection for near-zero query latency. Each dolt CLI invocation costs ~170ms in process startup. A persistent dolt sql-server connection via MySQL protocol would reduce per-query time to ~1-5ms. Nearly all CLI commands have SQL equivalents via Dolt system tables and stored procedures. The entire codebase routes through madolt--run in madolt-dolt.el, making incremental migration possible. This epic must be broken into sub-beads before implementation begins. See child issues for detailed breakdown.
close_reason: SQL server integration foundation complete: madolt-connection.el (connection manager), SQL translation registry in madolt--run, read-only SQL translations (status, branches, remotes, tags, log), mutation SQL translations (all DOLT_* stored procedures), process buffer SQL logging. Diff and prefetch migration closed separately as requiring deeper restructuring.

title: Cache log entries with parent hashes to avoid extra CLI call on commit expand
description: When expanding a commit in the log view, madolt-log--parent-hash makes a separate dolt log --parents call (~200ms). Instead, always request parent hashes in the initial log query and cache them, so commit expand only needs the diff call, not the parent lookup.
close_reason: Implemented --parents flag in madolt-log-entries, rewrote madolt-log--parent-hash to use cached entries. Log tab-expand: 4->3 CLI calls, revision buffer: 3->2 CLI calls.

title: Preserve point position after stage/unstage operations in status buffer
description: When staging or unstaging a table in the status buffer, point jumps away (typically to the top of the buffer). It should stay on or near the same logical position — e.g. if you stage a table, point should move to the next table entry, not jump to line 1. Magit preserves point across refresh by remembering the section at point and restoring it after re-rendering.
close_reason: Fixed point preservation by delegating to magit-section-goto-successor unconditionally, which handles same-section, opposite-section (staged↔unstaged), sibling, and parent fallbacks. Previously fell back to point-min when the section disappeared.

title: Improve demo tape: show help dialog, transient pauses, more features
description: The demo tape needs several improvements: (1) Start by showing the help/dispatch dialog via ? so viewers can see all available commands, (2) Linger longer whenever a transient pops up with multiple options so viewers can read them, (3) Show off more advanced features beyond basic staging/committing/log — e.g. diff viewing, SQL query, blame, branch operations, remote management. The demo should give a comprehensive overview of madolt capabilities.

title: Add "l a" for all-branches log (dolt log --all)

title: Show-more button: fix keybinding display and show next count
description: Two issues with the show-more buttons: 1) KEYBINDING: Button says M-x madolt-diff-double-limit because substitute-command-keys looks up the double-fn which has no keybinding. The + key is bound to madolt-show-more, not the per-module functions. 2) NEXT COUNT: Should show how many will be shown after pressing +, e.g. (100 of 1702 shown; next: 200).

title: Show-more button: fix keybinding display, show next count, add fixed-increment option
description:
Three issues with the show-more buttons:

1. KEYBINDING DISPLAY: The button says 'Type M-x madolt-diff-double-limit' because substitute-command-keys looks up the double-fn (e.g. madolt-diff-double-limit) which has no keybinding. The + key is bound to madolt-show-more, not to the per-module double-limit functions. Fix: pass madolt-show-more as the keybinding lookup function.

2. NEXT COUNT: Button shows '(100 of 1702 shown)' but doesn't indicate how many will be shown after pressing +. Should show something like '(100 of 1702 shown; next: 200)'.

3. DUAL ACTIONS: Currently only doubling is available. Add a second option for a fixed increment (e.g. +100 more). This could be two buttons/lines, or a second keybinding like '=' for fixed increment vs '+' for double.

title: Dispatch transient wastes space, needs more columns
description:
The help/dispatch transient (madolt-dispatch, bound to ? and h) is very wasteful of vertical space. It should use more columns to pack the commands more densely, reducing the height of the transient popup.

Currently the dispatch uses semantic groups ("Transient commands", "Transferring", "Inspecting", "Applying changes", "Essential commands") but each group is laid out in a single column, making the popup very tall.

Transient supports multi-column layouts via the :column property on groups. The dispatch should be reorganised to use 2-3 columns where appropriate, similar to how magit-dispatch arranges its commands.

File: madolt.el (transient-define-prefix madolt-dispatch)
close_reason: Merged Transferring into Transient commands as 3rd column. Shortened Essential labels. Reduced vertical height.

title: Make 'Recent commits' section conditional like magit's or-recent pattern
description:
Currently the status buffer always shows "Recent commits" at the bottom, even when unpushed/unpulled sections are present. This is redundant — if the user can see what is unpushed/unpulled, they already see the recent commit activity.

## Magit pattern

Magit uses magit-insert-unpulled-from-upstream-or-recent in its default status hook. This function:
1. If there is an upstream AND there are unpulled commits: shows "Unpulled from <upstream>" section
2. Otherwise (no upstream, or fully up-to-date): shows "Recent commits" as a fallback

Similarly magit-insert-unpushed-to-upstream-or-recent shows unpushed OR recent.

The net effect is that "Recent commits" only appears when there is nothing more specific to show (no remote configured, or fully synchronized).

## Current madolt behavior

madolt-status-sections-hook contains all three functions independently:
- madolt-insert-unpushed-commits (shows when ahead)
- madolt-insert-unpulled-commits (shows when behind)
- madolt-insert-recent-commits (always shows last 10)

## Desired behavior

Replace the three separate functions with conditional versions:
- If there are unpushed commits: show "Unpushed to <upstream>"
- If there are unpulled commits: show "Unpulled from <upstream>"
- If NEITHER unpushed nor unpulled sections were shown (no upstream, or fully synchronized): show "Recent commits" as fallback

This could be done either by:
1. A single combined function like magit's or-recent pattern, OR
2. Making madolt-insert-recent-commits check whether unpushed/unpulled sections were already inserted

## Files affected

- madolt-status.el (madolt-insert-recent-commits, madolt-status-sections-hook)
- test/madolt-status-tests.el (update tests for conditional behavior)
close_reason: Recent commits section now only shows when no unpushed/unpulled sections were inserted, matching magit's or-recent pattern. 2 new tests.

title: Add safety row limit to SQL query results
description:
The SQL query buffer currently renders ALL result rows with no default limit.

## Current code path

1. **Fetch**: madolt-sql-refresh-buffer (madolt-sql.el:82) calls madolt--run "sql" "-q" query "-r" "tabular" at line 85
2. **CLI command**: dolt sql -q "<user query>" -r tabular
3. **Rendering**: madolt-sql--insert-result (madolt-sql.el:103) inserts all lines via (dolist (line lines) ...) at line 105
4. **No limit**: The user controls the query and could add LIMIT themselves, but there is no safety net

## Implementation plan

This is the lowest priority since users write their own SQL and can add LIMIT clauses. However a safety limit could be added:

1. Add a defcustom madolt-sql-max-rows (default 1000, higher than other limits since SQL is explicit)
2. Either:
   a. Detect if the user's query already contains a LIMIT clause; if not, append one, OR
   b. Post-fetch: count result lines and truncate with a show-more button
3. Option (b) is simpler and doesn't risk breaking SQL syntax

## Files affected

- madolt-sql.el (truncation + show-more button)
- madolt-dolt.el (defcustom)
close_reason: Added safety row limit (1000 lines) to SQL results with show-more button. 3 new tests.

title: Table status faces need light/dark theme variants
description:
These madolt-specific faces have no magit equivalent and use hardcoded colors without light/dark variants:

| Face                  | Definition                     | Issue                                                   |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| madolt-table-modified | :foreground "yellow3"          | No light/dark variant; yellow on light bg is unreadable |
| madolt-table-new      | :foreground "green3"           | No light/dark variant                                   |
| madolt-table-deleted  | :foreground "red3"             | No light/dark variant                                   |
| madolt-table-renamed  | :foreground "cyan3"            | No light/dark variant                                   |
| madolt-table-conflict | :foreground "red" :weight bold | No light/dark variant                                   |

These are legitimately madolt-specific (magit does not have per-status-type colored faces for file entries -- it uses magit-diff-file-heading for all), but they need proper theme support.

Fix: Add (background light) / (background dark) display class variants to all these faces, similar to how magit defines its color faces. For example:

  (defface madolt-table-modified
    '((((class color) (background light)) :foreground "goldenrod4")
      (((class color) (background dark))  :foreground "yellow3"))
    "Face for modified table status.")

Choose colors that are readable on both light and dark backgrounds.

File: madolt-status.el
close_reason: All face alignment complete. Headers use plain text, headings use magit auto-face, process/diff/log faces match magit conventions, status faces have light/dark variants.

title: Log faces should inherit from magit equivalents
description:
In magit:
- magit-log-author: foreground firebrick (light) / tomato (dark), with explicit :slant normal :weight normal
- magit-log-date: foreground grey30 (light) / grey80 (dark), with explicit :slant normal :weight normal
- Ref decorations use per-ref-type faces: magit-tag, magit-branch-local, magit-branch-remote for individual ref types

In madolt:
- madolt-log-author: foreground cyan3 (hardcoded, no light/dark variants) -- completely different color family (cool blue-green vs warm red/orange)
- madolt-log-date: inherits shadow -- close but not identical; shadow varies by theme while magit has explicit grey values
- madolt-log-refs: foreground green3, :weight bold (hardcoded, no light/dark variants) -- magit uses per-ref-type faces instead of a single face for all refs

Fix:
1. Change madolt-log-author to inherit magit-log-author (or at least use similar warm colors with light/dark variants)
2. Change madolt-log-date to inherit magit-log-date
3. Ideally replace madolt-log-refs with per-ref-type faces following magit's pattern (magit-tag, magit-branch-local, magit-branch-remote), or at minimum add light/dark variants

title: Process faces should inherit magit-process-ok/ng
description:
In magit:
- magit-process-ok: inherits magit-section-heading, foreground green
- magit-process-ng: inherits magit-section-heading, foreground red

These inherit from magit-section-heading which provides :weight bold and the goldenrod foreground, then overridden with green/red.

The Emacs built-in success/error faces look different from magit's process faces (typically just green/red foreground without the bold/heading style).

title: Section headings should use magit-insert-heading auto-face
description:
In magit, section headings like "Staged changes", "Unstaged changes", "Untracked files" are inserted by passing plain unpropertized text to magit-insert-heading, which automatically applies the magit-section-heading face (foreground LightGoldenrod2 on dark, DarkGoldenrod4 on light, bold, extended). Child counts use the magit-section-child-count face and the magit-section-show-child-count mechanism.

In madolt, section headings are inserted as:
  (magit-insert-heading (propertize (format "Staged changes (%d)" count) 'font-lock-face 'madolt-section-heading))

Because the text is pre-propertized, magit-insert-heading sees existing face properties and inserts it as-is, bypassing its automatic face application and child-count mechanism.

madolt-section-heading inherits from magit-section-heading so the visual result is similar, but the approach is fragile -- it prevents magit-insert-heading from doing its own face management. If magit changes its heading insertion logic, madolt would not benefit.

Fix: Pass plain unpropertized text to magit-insert-heading and let it auto-apply the face. Use magit-insert-heading's built-in child-count mechanism instead of manually formatting "Staged changes (N)". Consider removing the madolt-section-heading face entirely if it adds nothing over magit-section-heading.

File: madolt-status.el
close_reason: All face alignment complete. Headers use plain text, headings use magit auto-face, process/diff/log faces match magit conventions, status faces have light/dark variants.

title: Header keywords should be plain text, not bold
description:
In magit, the header keyword labels ("Head: ", "Merge: ", "Upstream: ", "Push: ", "Tags: ") are inserted via (insert (format "%-10s" "Head: ")) with NO face property -- they are plain text. The entire header line sits inside a magit-insert-section, but the keywords themselves are unpropertized.

In madolt, the keyword labels ("Head:     ", "Database: ", "Path:     ", etc.) are propertized with 'font-lock-face 'bold in madolt-insert-status-header. This makes them visually heavier than magit's equivalent.

Fix: Remove the 'font-lock-face 'bold from all header keyword labels in madolt-insert-status-header. The keywords should be unstyled (inheriting the default face), matching magit's convention.

title: Align faces/colors with magit conventions
description:
Madolt's color scheme diverges from magit in several ways that make it look inconsistent:

1. **Header keywords** (Head:, Database:, etc.) are bold in madolt but plain text in magit
2. **Section headings** are pre-propertized instead of letting magit-insert-heading auto-apply faces
3. **Process faces** inherit from success/error instead of magit-process-ok/ng
4. **Diff context face** inherits shadow instead of magit-diff-context
5. **Diff table heading** adds :height 1.1 and lacks :extend t vs magit-diff-file-heading
6. **Log faces** use hardcoded cyan3/green3 instead of inheriting magit-log-author/date
7. **Table status faces** use hardcoded colors with no light/dark theme variants

Files: madolt-status.el, madolt-diff.el, madolt-log.el, madolt-process.el
close_reason: Replaced with individual beads per change

title: Status buffer header should show database name, filesystem location, and sql-server location
description: The status buffer header currently shows branch, HEAD commit, upstream, and remote info. It should also display: (1) the database name (directory basename), (2) the filesystem path to the database, and (3) the sql-server location if a dolt sql-server is running. This makes it immediately clear which database you're looking at, especially when working with multiple databases.
close_reason: Status header now shows Database name, Path, and Server (localhost:PORT + pid) when dolt sql-server is running. 7 new tests.

title: Commit should use buffer-based message editing like magit, with body support
description: Currently madolt-commit uses minibuffer input for commit messages, which doesn't support multi-line message bodies. Magit uses a dedicated buffer (COMMIT_EDITMSG) with with-editor for composing commit messages, which allows a summary line + blank line + body. However, dolt v1.82.x does NOT support $EDITOR-based commit messages (dolt commit without -m fails), so this would need to be a custom buffer that assembles the -m argument from the buffer contents. Should support: (1) dedicated commit message buffer with major mode, (2) summary + body separated by blank line, (3) C-c C-c to confirm, C-c C-k to cancel, (4) diff shown below the message area for reference.
close_reason: Implemented buffer-based commit message editing with madolt-commit-message-mode, C-c C-c/C-c C-k, diff reference, M-p/M-n history, amend support. 43 tests pass.

title: Create animated GIF demo of madolt features
description:
Create a realistic animated GIF demonstrating the most commonly used madolt features, starting with madolt-status. The demo should appear as if a human is interacting with it in real-time (natural typing speed, pauses for reading, etc.).

Features to demo (suggested order):
1. Opening madolt-status buffer - show the status overview with headers, sections
2. Navigating sections with TAB to expand/collapse table diffs
3. Viewing row-level diffs (added/modified/deleted rows with colour highlighting)
4. Staging tables (s key)
5. Committing changes (c c, type message, confirm)
6. Viewing the log (l l) with margin annotations
7. Expanding a log entry to see structured diff (TAB)
8. Using the diff transient (d d)
9. Unstaging (u key)

Technical approach:
- https://github.com/charmbracelet/vhs is almost certainly the best approach
- Probably emacsclient connecting to a dedicated test emacs server will help too
- Convert to GIF with appropriate frame timing
- Ensure terminal colours are visible (use a dark theme)
- Target ~30-60 seconds, reasonable resolution
close_reason: Created demo.tape VHS script and demo.gif (467K). Demonstrates: status buffer overview, inline cell-level diffs, staging tables, committing, log with margin annotations, and commit diff expansion.

title: Show unpushed/unpulled commits in status buffer
description:
Magit shows sections for unpushed and unpulled commits in the status buffer. Add similar sections for madolt:
- 'Unpushed to origin/main (N)' section showing commits ahead of remote
- 'Unpulled from origin/main (N)' section showing commits behind remote

Requires comparing local HEAD with remote tracking branch. dolt doesn't have a built-in way to show this without fetching first, but after a fetch, comparing log ranges (dolt log origin/main..HEAD and HEAD..origin/main) would work.

Add to madolt-status-sections-hook.
close_reason: Unpushed/unpulled commit sections in status buffer

title: Clean command: remove untracked tables
description:
Add a clean command (mapped to a key, maybe part of the apply group):
- dolt clean removes untracked tables from the working set
- Should prompt for confirmation with y-or-n-p listing the tables to be removed
- Could be a suffix in the dispatch transient or a standalone keybinding

This is the dolt equivalent of git clean.
close_reason: Clean command implemented: x key removes untracked tables with confirmation. Context-sensitive: works on individual table or section heading. 5 automated tests + interactive QA verified.

title: Tag transient: create, delete, list tags
description:
Add a tag transient (t) with suffixes:
- t t: create lightweight tag (dolt tag <name> [commit])
- t k: delete tag (dolt tag -d <name>, with confirmation)
- t l: list tags (dolt tag)

Tags should be shown in log buffer ref annotations (likely already handled if dolt log includes them). The main feature is interactive tag management.
close_reason: Tag transient implemented: t t (create), t k (delete with confirmation), t l (list), -m flag for annotated tags. Backend functions madolt-tag-names, madolt-tag-create, madolt-tag-delete in madolt-dolt.el. 20 tests, interactive QA passed.

title: Cherry-pick and revert commands
description:
Add commands for cherry-pick and revert:
- A: cherry-pick transient (dolt cherry-pick <commit>)
  - A A: cherry-pick a commit (with completing-read or manual hash entry)
- Revert could be part of the same or separate:
  - dolt revert <commit> (creates a new commit that undoes changes from the specified commit)

Both commands should prompt for the commit hash/ref. Process output should be shown in the process buffer.
close_reason: Cherry-pick (A) and revert (V) transients. Cherry-pick: A A (apply commit), A a (abort), --allow-empty flag. Revert: V V (revert commit). Both with integration tests. 17 tests, 326 total pass.

title: Merge transient: merge branches
description:
Add a merge transient (m) with suffixes:
- m m: merge branch into current (dolt merge <branch>, with completing-read)
- m a: abort merge (dolt merge --abort)

Should handle merge conflicts gracefully by showing conflict state in status buffer. Note: dolt merge does not support --squash or --no-ff like git does. Check dolt merge --help for available options.
close_reason: Merge transient implemented with m m (merge), m a (abort), and flags --no-ff, --ff-only, --squash, --no-commit. 14 tests, interactive QA passed.

title: Stash transient: create, apply, pop, drop stashes
description:
Add a stash transient (z or Z) with suffixes:
- z z: stash current changes (dolt stash)
- z p: pop most recent stash (dolt stash pop)
- z a: apply stash without removing (dolt stash apply)
- z k: drop a stash (dolt stash drop, with confirmation)
- z l: list stashes (already shown in status buffer)

The status buffer already displays stashes via madolt-insert-stashes. This adds interactive commands to create and manage them.
close_reason: Stash transient implemented: z z (stash), z p (pop), z k (drop), z x (clear), z l (list), --include-untracked and --all flags. 17 tests, 343/343 suite. Interactive QA verified all operations.

title: Make madolt-process buffer layout match magit-process style
description: The madolt-process buffer should more closely match magit-process layout: chevron in the left margin followed by exit code and then the command. Currently the layout differs from what magit users expect.
close_reason: Process buffer now matches magit-process layout: right-justified exit code followed by command. Tested both automated (203 tests, 199 pass) and interactive (tmux verification).

title: Show 'dolt doesn't support staging individual rows' instead of 'Nothing to stage here'
description: When trying to stage/unstage a row section, the message 'Nothing to stage here' is misleading. Instead show 'dolt doesn't support staging individual rows' (or similar for unstaging). Handle all similar scenarios where row-level operations are attempted.
close_reason: Implemented row-level error messages for stage/unstage/discard with 5 new tests

title: Fix README license - should be GPL-3.0-or-later, not MIT
description: The README states MIT license but all source files use SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later. The README needs to be corrected.
close_reason: False alarm - README already correctly states GPL-3.0-or-later.

title: RET on commit in status buffer should replace buffer, not split window
description: Investigating... When pressing RET on a commit in the status buffer, it opens the revision buffer in a new window alongside the status buffer. The user expects it to replace the status buffer in the same window, similar to how they have magit configured. Need to investigate how madolt-display-buffer works vs magit's display-buffer-alist approach.
close_reason: madolt-display-buffer now delegates to magit-display-buffer when available, respecting the user's magit-display-buffer-function setting.

title: Schema changes under table headers should be indented for visual hierarchy
description: In the status buffer, when a table section (e.g. 'new table tasks') is expanded, the 'Schema changes' heading and its SQL content appear at the same indentation level as the table heading itself. They should be indented further to make it visually clear they belong under that table.
close_reason: Schema changes and row diffs under table headers in the status buffer are now indented at 6 spaces (vs 2 for the table heading) via the madolt-diff--indent dynamic variable.

title: Row diffs should distinguish between visibility level 3 (summary) and level 4 (detail)
description: In both the status view and the log view, row diffs should behave differently at magit-section visibility level 3 vs level 4. At level 3, each row change should show as a single summary line. At level 4, it should expand to show per-field details across multiple lines (as the log view already does). Currently there is no distinction between these two levels.
close_reason: Row diffs now distinguish between level 3 (summary) and level 4 (detail) in both status and log views. Status buffer now uses row-diff sections with collapsible bodies instead of plain text. Also shows schema changes inline in status buffer.

title: Commit/revision view lacks syntax colouring
description: The commit/revision buffer (shown via RET on a log entry) has lacking colouring compared to the diff views. Diff output should have proper face colouring for added/removed/modified rows, headers, and metadata.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt-log--find-entry now queries dolt directly for the specific hash instead of searching current branch history. Added madolt-diff-schema face for SQL statements in schema diff sections. Applied madolt-log-author and madolt-log-date faces to revision metadata.

title: Log view TAB expansion should match status buffer table expansion style
description: When expanding a commit in the log view with TAB, the expanded content should look similar to expanding tables in the madolt-status sections (structured diff display). Currently it shows --stat text output.
close_reason: Log view TAB expansion now shows structured per-table diffs with row-level and cell-level changes, matching the status buffer style. Replaced --stat text output with madolt-diff--insert-table-diff rendering.

title: g keybinding may prompt instead of directly refreshing
description: The spec binds g to madolt-refresh directly. The implementation inherits g from special-mode-map which calls revert-buffer. revert-buffer may prompt on non-file buffers. Magit explicitly binds g to magit-refresh. We should do the same.
close_reason: Fixed: g explicitly bound to madolt-refresh instead of inheriting revert-buffer from special-mode-map

title: Inline diff in status buffer always shows unstaged diff even for staged tables
description: madolt--wash-table-diff calls madolt-diff-insert-table which runs dolt diff -r json TABLE without --staged. Tables in the Staged changes section show their unstaged diff rather than their staged diff. Need to pass --staged when the parent section type is staged.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt--wash-table-diff now detects parent section type and passes staged flag to madolt-diff-insert-table

title: Eager diff fetching on status refresh instead of lazy on TAB
description: madolt--wash-table-diff is called inside magit-insert-section-body during refresh, meaning every table diff is fetched when the buffer refreshes, not lazily when TAB expands the section. For databases with many changed tables this causes unnecessary dolt diff calls. Should use a proper washer for deferred loading.
close_reason: Not a bug: sections are created with HIDE=t and magit-insert-section-body already defers body evaluation as a washer when section is hidden. Diffs are only fetched on TAB expansion.

title: madolt-diff--build-args reverses revision order
description: In madolt-diff.el madolt-diff--build-args, push is used to prepend rev-a then rev-b to the args list, resulting in (rev-b rev-a ...) which swaps the from..to order of the diff.
close_reason: Fixed: reversed push order in madolt-diff--build-args so rev-a comes before rev-b

title: Usability review: compare implementation against MVP spec and file issues for gaps
description:
Do a thorough usability review of the existing madolt mode:

1. Compare the implementation against specs/MVP.md to identify unimplemented features
2. Evaluate overall usability — how useful is it? What's awkward or confusing?
3. File individual beads for anything found (bugs, missing features, UX improvements)
4. Fix issues where practical

Key areas to audit:
- Status buffer layout vs spec (Remote: line, section ordering)
- Diff viewer (structured vs raw mode toggle, faces, section hierarchy)
- Log viewer (margin display, stat expansion, revision buffer)
- Commit workflow (with-editor integration, message mode, assertion)
- Keybindings completeness vs spec
- Overall polish and edge cases
close_reason: Audit complete, 11 issues filed from usability review

title: Unstaged changes should appear before staged changes in status buffer
description: In the madolt status buffer, unstaged changes currently appear after staged changes. The conventional order (matching magit) is unstaged first, then staged, so users see what still needs attention before what's already prepared for commit.
close_reason: Swapped order of madolt-insert-unstaged-changes and madolt-insert-staged-changes in madolt-status-sections-hook to match magit convention. All 190 tests pass (4 pre-existing failures unrelated).

title: Move developer info to CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Move developer-facing information (build/test instructions, interactive QA testing guide, technical notes) from AGENTS.md to a new CONTRIBUTING.md file. AGENTS.md should retain only AI agent-specific instructions.
close_reason: Moved project overview, build/test, interactive QA testing, and technical notes to CONTRIBUTING.md. AGENTS.md now references it.

title: Interactive test: refresh and buffer lifecycle
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press g to refresh status buffer, verify it updates. Make changes outside emacs (via dolt CLI), press g, verify new state is reflected. Press q to quit, verify buffer is buried. Reopen with madolt-status, verify buffer is reused.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: g refreshes and picks up external dolt changes, q buries buffer, re-opening reuses existing buffer, multiple refresh/navigation cycles produce no post-command-hook errors.

title: Interactive test: process buffer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: perform some dolt operations (stage, refresh), then press $ to show process buffer. Verify it shows the dolt CLI commands that were run with their output and exit codes.
close_reason: All 3 checks pass: $ opens process buffer showing all dolt CLI commands with exit codes and output, buffer in madolt-process-mode, entries are magit-section process sections.

title: Interactive test: discard changes
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: modify data in test db, verify unstaged changes appear, navigate to the table and press k (discard), verify confirmation prompt appears, confirm, verify changes are discarded and table disappears from unstaged section.
close_reason: Discard works: confirmation prompt appears (yes-or-no-p), answering yes discards changes, table disappears from unstaged section, data reverted in dolt. Note: use tmux send-keys for interactive prompts, not cl-letf.

title: Interactive test: dispatch menu and navigation
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press ? to show dispatch, verify all three groups visible (Transient commands, Applying changes, Essential commands). Test section navigation: n/p to move between sections, TAB to toggle sections, RET on table to visit it.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: ? shows all 3 dispatch groups with correct keys, n navigates forward through all section types, p navigates backward, TAB toggles section collapse/expand with visual ellipsis.

title: Interactive test: log viewer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press l l to show current branch log, verify log buffer opens with commit entries showing hash/date/author/message. Test TAB to expand a commit (shows stat). Test RET on a commit to open revision buffer with full diff.
close_reason: All 5 checks pass: l l opens log with heading 'Commits on main:', commit entries show hash/refs/date/author/message, TAB toggles stat details with visual ellipsis, RET opens revision buffer with full commit metadata and diff.

title: Interactive test: diff viewer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: modify data in test db, press d d to show working tree diff, verify diff buffer opens showing table name and row changes with cell-level detail. Test d s (staged diff). Test TAB to expand/collapse diff sections.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: d d opens diff buffer with table sections for both modified and new tables, row changes show +/- markers with cell details, staged diff (d s) shows only staged tables, diff buffer in correct mode.

title: Interactive test: commit workflow
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: stage changes, press c c to open commit transient then create commit, verify minibuffer prompts for message, provide message, verify commit succeeds and status buffer refreshes to clean state. Test c a (amend) as well.
close_reason: All 3 checks pass: commit creates commit with minibuffer message, status refreshes to clean state after commit, amend pre-fills old message and creates new commit with updated hash.

title: Interactive test: staging and unstaging tables
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: create a new table or modify data so unstaged changes appear, verify unstaged section shows the table, press s to stage it, verify it moves to staged section, press u to unstage, verify it moves back. Test S (stage all) and U (unstage all).
close_reason: All 7 checks pass: unstaged changes appear correctly, s stages table at point, u unstages, S stages all, U unstages all, untracked tables shown separately, staging untracked works.

title: Interactive test: status buffer basics
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: open madolt-status in test dolt db, verify header shows branch name and HEAD commit, verify recent commits section visible, verify clean status has no staged/unstaged/untracked sections.
close_reason: All 7 checks pass: header shows branch+commit, recent commits section visible with 2 entries, no staged/unstaged/untracked sections on clean db, correct mode hierarchy, correct default-directory, mode line shows Madolt Status.

title: Make ? show keybinding help like magit does
description: The current madolt-dispatch (bound to ?) only shows 5 top-level transient commands (Status, Diff, Log, Commit, Process). Magit's dispatch shows a comprehensive grid of ALL available keybindings organized by category: transient commands, applying changes (stage/unstage/discard), and essential commands (refresh, quit, toggle, visit). Update madolt-dispatch to mirror this pattern so ? gives a complete keybinding reference.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive dispatch menu with three groups: transient commands, applying changes, and essential commands. 9 new tests added.

title: madolt-rebase-in-progress-p silently fails when SQL server is enabled
description: madolt-rebase-in-progress-p calls (madolt-dolt-string "status") and checks for "rebase in progress" in the output. When madolt-use-sql-server is enabled, the status query is routed through SQL returning tab-separated dolt_status rows (e.g. users\t1\tmodified) which never contain that string. Rebase detection silently returns nil. Fix: bypass SQL routing for this call, since dolt_status has no concept of rebase state.
close_reason: Changed madolt-rebase-in-progress-p to use madolt--run-cli directly instead of madolt-dolt-string, bypassing SQL routing since dolt_status system table has no rebase state

title: E i (server status) should show hostname before port
description: madolt-server-status message shows 'Connected to sql-server on port N' but should include the hostname, e.g. 'Connected to sql-server on 127.0.0.1:N'.
close_reason: Server status now shows hostname:port

title: Refs buffer header-line has leading space in 'Comparing with HEAD'
description: The refs buffer header-line-format includes a leading space in the propertized string: (format " Comparing with %s" ...). The leading space should be removed.
close_reason: Fixed in refs buffer commit: visibility level 2, ahead/behind layout, text properties, header space

title: Re-render the demo .gif
description: Re-render the demo .gif to reflect current UI changes (ref labels, dispatch layout, SQL server transient, etc.). Requires human review before pushing.

title: Sort dispatch transient alphabetically and minimize lines
description: The Transient commands section of madolt-dispatch is unordered. Sort entries alphabetically within each column and arrange columns to use the minimum number of lines needed.
close_reason: Dispatch transient sorted alphabetically, Inspecting and Applying changes rendered side by side

title: Rearrange help transient: put Inspecting and Applying changes side by side
description: In the dispatch transient (madolt-dispatch), put the Inspecting and Applying changes groups side by side to save vertical space, rather than stacking them vertically.
close_reason: Dispatch transient sorted alphabetically, Inspecting and Applying changes rendered side by side

title: refs: add sections hook for customizable section order
description: Magit uses a customizable magit-refs-sections-hook (default: error-header, branch-description, local-branches, remote-branches, tags). Madolt hardcodes the three inserters directly in madolt-refs-refresh-buffer. Should use a hook variable with madolt-run-section-hook.
close_reason: Added madolt-refs-sections-hook defcustom (default: local-branches, remote-branches, tags). Refactored inserters to be zero-arg functions using buffer-local data vars. Users can customize section order or remove sections. 2 new tests.

title: refs: show tag annotation message instead of hash
description: Madolt shows 8-char hash for tags. Magit shows the tag annotation message instead (processed through magit-log--wash-summary). Need to parse dolt tag -v output to extract tag messages and display them instead of hashes.
close_reason: Tag annotation messages parsed and displayed instead of hashes

title: refs: add light/dark background variants to faces
description: Madolt refs faces use single hardcoded foreground colors. Magit defines separate colors for (background light) and (background dark). Affects madolt-branch-local (should be SkyBlue4 on light), madolt-branch-remote (DarkOliveGreen4 on light), madolt-tag (Goldenrod4 on light, LightGoldenrod2 on dark instead of Khaki).
close_reason: Added light/dark background variants to all refs faces

title: refs: strip remote prefix from remote branch names by default
description: Magit strips the remote prefix by default (controlled by magit-refs-show-remote-prefix), showing just 'main' instead of 'origin/main'. Madolt always shows the full 'origin/main'. Add a defcustom to control this, defaulting to stripped.
close_reason: Added defcustom, defaults to stripped prefix matching magit

title: refs: show remote URL in remote section heading
description: Magit shows 'Remote origin (git@github.com:...)' including the fetch URL. Madolt shows only 'Remote origin:'. Should include the URL from madolt-remotes alist.
close_reason: Remote URL now shown in section heading

title: Document CLI-only fallbacks (blame, raw diff, stash verification)
description: Identify and document all commands that must remain CLI-only: (1) dolt blame: no SQL equivalent, no system table. Could theoretically be reimplemented via dolt_history_<table> queries but that's complex and low priority. Keep as CLI. (2) Raw tabular diff mode: the formatted | +| <| >| output is CLI-specific. Keep as CLI fallback in madolt-diff.el. (3) dolt stash: verify whether DOLT_STASH() stored procedure exists in current Dolt version. If not, document as CLI-only. (4) dolt reflog with --all flag: verify dolt_reflog system table supports equivalent filtering. (5) dolt log --graph: verify if there's any SQL equivalent for graph rendering. (6) Update AGENTS.md and code comments to document which operations use SQL vs CLI and why.
close_reason: Documented CLI-only fallbacks and SQL-routable operations in AGENTS.md Technical Notes section. Verified: dolt blame (no SQL), dolt diff (CLI-specific rendering), dolt log --graph (CLI-only), dolt stash (DOLT_STASH exists but different API), dolt reflog (no dolt_reflog system table in v1.82.x). Added tables listing all CLI-only and SQL-routable operations.

title: Render graph for non-linear log history (--graph with multi-branch)
description: The log transient has a --graph argument but it is unclear whether the graph rendering works correctly for non-linear history (merges, multiple branches). Need to investigate how dolt log --graph outputs data, and ensure madolt renders ASCII graph lines correctly when viewing all-branches log. This may require parsing graph prefixes from dolt log output and rendering them with appropriate faces.
close_reason: Implemented graph rendering for --graph flag in log buffer. Graph prefix characters (* | / \) are now captured during parsing and rendered with madolt-log-graph face. Junction lines (|/ for merge joins) are detected via second-pass analysis and rendered between commit sections. Works correctly with merge history, --all flag, and interactively verified in tmux.

title: Add M C for configuring remotes (URL)
description: Add M C keybinding for configuring remotes, similar to magit's remote configuration. Since dolt remotes only have a URL as configurable property (no fetch refspec, pushRemote, etc.), a full submenu may be overkill — a simple 'set URL' action via minibuffer prompt might suffice. Needs investigation into what dolt remote properties are actually configurable.
close_reason: Added C (Configure URL) action to madolt-remote-manage transient. Implements remove+re-add since dolt has no set-url. Also added origin remote to demo setup script.

title: Remove unhelpful '(N cell changed)' annotation from status buffer row summaries
description: The inline diff row summaries in the status buffer show '(1 cell changed)' etc. which doesn't add useful information since the expanded detail already shows exactly which cells changed. Consider removing or replacing with something more informative.
close_reason: Summary no longer shows '(N cell changed)' — replaced with inline old→new values for changed fields, with PK first

title: Add X keybinding for dolt reset transient
description: Magit binds X to magit-reset (hard/soft/mixed reset transient). Dolt supports 'dolt reset' with --soft and --hard flags. Adding this would match magit conventions and provide an important missing operation.
close_reason: Reset transient implemented with soft/hard/mixed commands, 17 passing tests, integrated into dispatch (X), keymap, and Makefile

title: Show merge conflicts section in status buffer
description: During merge conflicts, magit shows a dedicated 'Merge Conflicts' section in the status buffer. Madolt has a separate C command for conflicts but doesn't show them inline in the status buffer. Adding a conflict section to madolt-status-sections-hook would improve discoverability during merge workflows.
close_reason: Merge conflicts section added to status buffer, parses Unmerged paths from dolt status, 4 tests

title: Preserve section visibility across status buffer refreshes
description: When a user expands a table diff with TAB, pressing g (refresh) resets all sections to their default visibility. Magit preserves expanded/collapsed state via magit-section-set-visibility-hook. Integrating this would significantly improve the UX for users who frequently refresh while exploring diffs.
close_reason: Added magit-section-show root call after refresh to apply cached visibility overlays, 2 tests

title: Show commit from log buffer (RET already works but improve metadata)
description:
When pressing RET on a commit in the log buffer, madolt-show-commit opens a revision buffer. The metadata section could be improved:
- Show parent commit hash(es)
- Show diff stat summary (files changed, insertions, deletions equivalent for tables)
- Show the full commit message (currently only first line)
- Better formatting of the metadata header

This is an enhancement to the existing madolt-show-commit / madolt-revision-mode.
close_reason: Improved commit detail view: parent hash(es), Merge: line for merge commits, diff stat summary (per-table row counts), full multi-line commit message. Also parses Merge: line in madolt-log-entries adding :parents plist key. 12 new tests, 355/355 suite. Interactive QA verified all three commit types (merge, normal, initial).

title: Remote management: add, remove, rename remotes
description:
Add commands for managing remotes:
- M a: add remote (dolt remote add <name> <url>)
- M k: remove remote (dolt remote remove <name>, with confirmation)
- M r: rename remote (dolt remote rename <old> <new>)

The Remote: header in the status buffer already shows configured remotes. This adds interactive management commands. Could be part of the push/fetch transient or a standalone remote transient.
close_reason: Remote management transient (M): add (M a) and remove (M k) remotes. Note: dolt has no 'remote rename' subcommand so rename was omitted. Backend: madolt-remote-add, madolt-remote-remove in madolt-dolt.el. -f flag for fetch-after-add. 13 new tests, 368/368 suite. Interactive QA verified add/remove with status buffer refresh.

title: Rebase transient
description:
Add a rebase transient (r) with suffixes:
- r r: rebase current branch onto another (dolt rebase <branch>)
- r a: abort rebase (dolt rebase --abort)
- r c: continue rebase (dolt rebase --continue)

Note: dolt rebase may not support interactive mode (-i). Check dolt rebase --help for available options. This is lower priority since rebase is an advanced operation.
close_reason: Implemented rebase transient with 13 passing tests

title: Reflog buffer
description:
Add a reflog command to view the history of ref changes:
- dolt reflog shows the history of HEAD updates
- Could be accessible from the log transient or dispatch
- Would use a dedicated buffer similar to the log buffer
- Shows hash, ref, and action for each entry

Lower priority since reflog is rarely needed in normal workflows.
close_reason: Implemented reflog buffer with 16 passing tests, accessible from log transient

title: Conflict resolution UI for merge conflicts
description:
When a merge results in conflicts, dolt stores them in special system tables. Add a UI for viewing and resolving conflicts:
- Show conflicts in the status buffer (dolt conflicts shows conflict state)
- Provide commands to resolve conflicts:
  - dolt conflicts resolve <table> marks a table's conflicts as resolved
  - dolt conflicts cat shows the conflicting rows
- May need a dedicated conflict buffer showing from/to/base values

This depends on the merge transient being implemented first.
close_reason: Implemented conflict resolution UI with transient, view, and resolve commands. 17 tests passing.

title: SQL query interface
description:
Add an interactive SQL query interface:
- Could be invoked from a keybinding or dispatch transient suffix
- Opens a SQL input buffer (or uses minibuffer for simple queries)
- Runs dolt sql -q and displays results in a tabular buffer
- Consider using sql-mode or a custom mode for input
- Results could use a dedicated result buffer with column alignment

This is a distinctive dolt feature not found in git/magit. Lower priority since users can always use dolt sql directly in a shell.
close_reason: Implemented SQL query interface with 14 passing tests, bound to 'e'

title: Blame mode: show per-row blame annotations
description:
Add a blame command that shows which commit last modified each row of a table:
- dolt blame <table> shows revision/author/timestamp per row
- Could be accessible via a keybinding or from the dispatch transient
- Would need a dedicated blame buffer/mode with syntax highlighting
- Consider whether to show as a table or as annotated rows

This is lower priority since blame is less commonly used interactively.
close_reason: Implemented blame mode with 12 passing tests, bound to 'B'

title: Enumerate dolt/magit functionality and file beads for missing features
description: Systematically enumerate all dolt CLI and magit UI functionality, determine which features make sense for dolt (bearing in mind dolt doesn't support all the same things as git), and file individual beads for each missing feature that should be implemented in madolt.
close_reason: Filed 15 beads covering all missing dolt/magit features: 2 P1 (branch, push/pull/fetch), 6 P2 (stash, merge, cherry-pick/revert, tag, clean, unpushed/unpulled), 7 P3 (blame, SQL, conflict resolution, reflog, rebase, remote mgmt, commit detail improvements)

title: Untracked tables should appear first in status buffer
description: In magit, untracked files appear before unstaged changes. The madolt status buffer should follow the same convention, showing untracked tables first, then unstaged changes, then staged changes.
close_reason: Moved madolt-insert-untracked-tables before madolt-insert-unstaged-changes in madolt-status-sections-hook, matching magit convention.

title: Bulk discard triggers multiple refreshes
description: madolt-discard on the unstaged heading calls madolt-run-dolt for each child table, each triggering a full madolt-refresh. Should use madolt-call-dolt for all but the last, or batch the operation.
close_reason: Fixed: bulk discard now uses madolt-call-dolt for each table and only refreshes once at the end

title: Status buffer missing Remote: header line
description: The MVP spec shows a Remote: line in the status header (Remote: origin https://dolthub.com/...) but madolt-insert-status-header only shows the Head: line. Add remote info display.
close_reason: Added Remote: header line to status buffer. Added madolt-remotes function to parse dolt remote -v output, and madolt-branch-remote face.

title: Discard uses yes-or-no-p instead of y-or-n-p
description: The MVP spec says discard prompts with y-or-n-p but the implementation uses yes-or-no-p which requires typing yes/no instead of just y/n. More conservative but deviates from spec.
close_reason: Fixed: changed yes-or-no-p to y-or-n-p per spec

title: Log viewer uses inline text instead of right margin for author/date
description: The MVP spec describes author name and date in a right margin area (similar to magit-log, configurable via madolt-log-margin). The implementation shows them inline in the heading text.
close_reason: Log viewer now uses Emacs right-margin overlays for author/date display, matching magit-log style. Added madolt-log-margin-width and madolt-log-author-width customization options.

title: Log transient missing --graph argument
description: The MVP spec includes (-g "Graph" "--graph") in the log transient but the implementation omits it.
close_reason: Added --graph to log transient. Also strip graph decoration from dolt log output and handle refs attached without space in --graph mode.

title: Show-more: discuss and possibly add dual action (double vs fixed increment)
description: Currently show-more only doubles the limit. Consider adding a second action for a fixed increment (e.g. +100 more). Could be two buttons/lines, or a second keybinding like = for fixed increment vs + for double. Needs design discussion before implementation.

title: Add w keybinding to copy section value to kill ring
description: Magit binds w to magit-copy-section-value which copies the current section's value (e.g. commit hash, table name) to the kill ring. This is a frequently-used convenience feature that would be easy to implement.
close_reason: Added madolt-copy-section-value bound to w, copies table name/commit hash to kill ring, 4 tests

title: Audit UX against magit for improvement opportunities
description: Audit the madolt UX against magit, which is the gold standard for this kind of interface. Look for any opportunities to improve by copying the style, design, interaction patterns, visual layout, keybindings, or anything else from magit. This includes buffer appearance, section rendering, transient menus, navigation feel, error handling UX, and any other aspects where magit sets the bar.
close_reason: Completed UX audit: restructured dispatch into semantic groups, added Upstream header line, fixed section order, normalized padding. Filed 4 follow-up issues for lower-priority improvements.
